261816.explanation: The municipality decides on this on a case-by-case basis. As far as GroenLinks-PvdA is concerned, this should remain so, and we support green entrepreneurship where possible.

261817.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes the municipality must ensure clean and healthy air for its residents. That is why we support a ban on wood burning during poor weather conditions.

261818.explanation: Sports are healthy. GroenLinks-PvdA believes sports should be accessible to everyone, regardless of income. We want to prevent residents from having to choose between a sports club membership and their child's music lessons.

261820.explanation: Zutphen currently has too few enforcement officers (BOAs). GroenLinks-PvdA therefore wants more BOAs in Zutphen.

261821.explanation: Prevention is better than cure. Organizing a weekly consultation hour contributes to police accessibility.

261822.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA is cautious about deploying camera surveillance. The mayor can use camera surveillance in Zutphen where necessary. In Zutphen, it has been agreed that camera surveillance is only used if all other means have proven inadequate.

261823.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA focuses on connection. Antisocial behavior in traffic does not belong.

261824.explanation: As GroenLinks-PvdA, we want the youth growing up in Zutphen to also be able to build their future in Zutphen.

261825.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that a baton can lead to violence.

261826.explanation: The city center of Zutphen is much more livable when the markets and the 's-Gravenhof are car-restricted. We therefore focus on parking on the edge of the center. This keeps the center car-restricted and easily accessible.

261827.explanation: We fully commit to more affordable housing. That is why GroenLinks-PvdA supports at least 35% social rental housing in new construction.

261828.explanation: More car-restricted streets and markets are good for the city center economy and make our city center even more attractive and livable.

261829.explanation: This must be considered on an individual basis. There is a big difference between personal and business debts. Entrepreneurship remains entrepreneurship. The municipality cannot guarantee everything.

261830.explanation: The prices for municipal permits in Zutphen are already very low.

261831.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants more space for cycling and walking. If driving speeds are reduced, it is quieter, cleaner, and safer.

261832.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes residents should have a voice in major decisions. We prefer citizens' councils because they foster connection, whereas referenda often lead to polarization. We choose connection.

261833.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants the youngest generation to have a voice as well.

261834.explanation: Meetings in the neighborhoods are more accessible. This brings politics closer to the residents.

261836.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds this measure too general. Targeted support is much more effective in our view.

261837.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that sustainability, circularity, and local entrepreneurship should go hand in hand in construction. Fortunately, this is already taken into account in tenders.

261838.explanation: We do not give priority, but we do strive for even faster processing of permit applications.

261839.explanation: For GroenLinks-PvdA, the principle is that the polluter pays. This creates a positive incentive toward less waste. For residents with low incomes, there are exemption schemes.

261840.explanation: In consultation with the respective hospitality businesses and per event, deviations from the normal times could be allowed.

261841.explanation: As GroenLinks-PvdA, we stand for an open and welcoming city. If Zutphen is asked to provide extra shelter, we want to consult our residents through a citizens' council. This way, we work toward a suitable solution for everyone.

261842.explanation: As GroenLinks-PvdA, we stand for an open and welcoming city. If Zutphen is asked to provide extra shelter, we want to consult our residents through a citizens' council. This way, we work toward a suitable solution for everyone.

261843.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that extremely vulnerable groups such as financially vulnerable single parents, victims of domestic violence, and refugees without a social network should be given priority.

261844.explanation: A fireworks show causes a lot of fine particles, waste, and stress for humans and animals. A sustainable alternative fits better with a healthy living environment and Zutphen's climate and environmental goals.

261846.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to relieve the energy grid and make local energy supply financially accessible for everyone.

261847.explanation: Community centers are important for social interaction, youth work, and welfare. Staying open in the evenings also strengthens social cohesion, prevents loneliness, and provides safe places for activities in the neighborhood.

261848.explanation: A grey bin should only remain where it really cannot be otherwise. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to focus more on waste separation and reducing residual waste.

261849.explanation: Organic farmers work without artificial fertilizers and chemical pesticides. Priority in leasing municipal land stimulates sustainable agriculture, protects soil and water, and contributes to healthy food production. This way, we continue working on BioCity Zutphen.

261850.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports an attractive swimming offer in Zutphen. This also includes a fun play area in the swimming pool. In addition, we want to broadly invest in public spaces where children can play.

261851.explanation: We want to implement the Clean Air Agreement, which means we strive for always clean air for everyone. Easter fires do not fit in with that. They have been forbidden since 2011.

261852.explanation: Green neighborhoods are beautiful neighborhoods. GroenLinks-PvdA wants green, sustainable, climate-adaptive neighborhoods. As the municipality of Zutphen, we already build quite nature-inclusive. We want to continue doing so.

261853.explanation: Plans for a new Hanzehof are already well underway. Additionally, Zutphen deserves a full-fledged cultural center with beautiful performances for everyone and cultural lessons for young and old.

261854.explanation: There is not enough to do for young people in Zutphen yet. That is why we want a youth center for young people between 15 and 25 years old. Young people decide themselves what will happen there.

261855.explanation: Sport is important and should be encouraged, but we prefer targeted subsidies for people who cannot afford it themselves.

261856.explanation: A university of applied sciences is not realistic for Zutphen. Apeldoorn, Deventer, Velp, and Arnhem are too close. We want to focus on strengthening vocational education (MBO) in Zutphen together with the business community. Craftsmanship has a long tradition in Zutphen and is no less valuable than higher education.

261857.explanation: Mastering the Dutch language is very important for equal opportunities. Starting behind at primary school is difficult to catch up on, so we start with toddlers.

261858.explanation: We want to prevent illegal waste dumping. The municipality must ensure that underground waste containers are emptied regularly.

261859.explanation: We will first continue building in Leesten-Oost and De Hoven Noord.

261860.explanation: We consider having our own housing company unwise. We support close cooperation with existing housing corporations to steer on affordability, sustainability, and quality. This aligns with our choice to build for residents, not for the market.

261861.explanation: Long-term vacancy is unacceptable. By fining owners who leave houses vacant for more than a year, we stimulate better use of existing housing. This is an effective way to make homes available faster without extra pressure on scarce space and nature.

261863.explanation: We find a moving allowance too general and costly a measure. Instead of moving allowances, we focus on suitable housing, shared housing, living together, and lifetime-proof living. This improves housing flow without coercion and with respect for everyone’s living situation.

261864.explanation: Multigenerational homes and caregiver homes fit our vision of care, connectedness, and communities where people look out for each other. By structurally including this in large new-build projects, we respond to changing housing wishes and strengthen social cohesion in neighborhoods.

261865.explanation: Collective building gives residents more control, provides affordable homes, and strengthens mutual connections. Therefore, GroenLinks-PvdA wants to designate locations for housing collectives and support these initiatives. This aligns with our commitment to participation and building together with residents.

261866.explanation: For GroenLinks-PvdA, high-rise buildings are possible but not in the historic center. Outside the historic center, considering sightlines and the local situation, it can be allowed. This must be done nature-inclusive because a healthy and green living environment is important everywhere.

261867.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants tenants in Zutphen to have access to an accessible and visible tenant team. This team helps with complaints about excessive rents, delayed maintenance, discrimination, and other abuses. This way we protect tenants and ensure decent living conditions.

261868.explanation: Trust must be central. With problematic debts, we want residents to gain peace and overview as soon as possible. By taking over debts and working with one debt and one point of contact, we reduce stress and increase the chances of a successful future without debts.

261869.explanation: To successfully become part of our Zutphen society, it is important that residents can keep their heads above water. That is why we support investing in lessons on managing money wisely.

261871.explanation: We want the children in our municipality to grow up healthy. Healthy nutrition is hugely important for healthy growth. We therefore consider it a good idea to help parents with young children with healthy nutrition. We do this by offering a free vegetable bag.

261872.explanation: We are not fundamentally opposed to an underground parking garage. Underground garages are very expensive to build, so first other solutions must be seriously considered. For example, the parking garage at the station and the parking lot at Houtwal could be better utilized.

261873.explanation: The ‘s Gravenhof is a special place in Zutphen’s history, and it should also become a special place in today’s Zutphen. We therefore support making the ‘s Gravenhof car-free. We do this together with Zutphen’s entrepreneurs and city center residents.

261874.explanation: A good set of teeth is very important for everyone’s health. Unfortunately, it is not self-evident for everyone to simply visit a dentist. That is why we want a dental bus to drive by schools.

261876.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants more youth workers because they are urgently needed to combat mental health problems among young people.

261816.explanation: Stimulating the local economy is good for the municipality, its residents, and the farmers themselves.

261817.explanation: We will always have to find a balance between healthy air and lungs and the cozy crackling of a fireplace. This also limits barbecues and fire baskets. Enforcement is impossible; education is better. A ban on wood stoves for new houses and vulnerable monuments is logical.

261818.explanation: This is already partly provided for in the Participation scheme. Burgerbelang also wants to enable discounts through a Zutphen pass on, among other things, sports. We want to work with sports clubs to look at effective arrangements. The municipality can certainly contribute financially.

261820.explanation: Burgerbelang wants to increase the feeling of safety by deploying more enforcement officers. They should focus mainly on preventing and controlling unsafe situations on the street and nuisance from, among other things, drug trafficking.

261821.explanation: It is good to look at which neighborhoods this would fit in.

261822.explanation: Burgerbelang has been advocating for broader use of cameras for years. The feeling of insecurity is high, and the police now have too few resources. Camera surveillance should also apply to the entire nightlife area.

261823.explanation: Burgerbelang observes unsafe and antisocial behavior with all kinds of vehicles. Discriminating based solely on fatbikes is unwise and legally not possible. Burgerbelang advocates enforcement against all antisocial behavior in traffic.

261824.explanation: We may now reserve 50% of these homes for Zutphen residents or the teachers, police officers, and nurses who work in Zutphen or Warnsveld. Let’s implement this as soon as possible and help our residents.

261825.explanation: That can only happen if the enforcement officer is properly trained, which currently is not the case for most officers.

261826.explanation: Aging also means more people have mobility issues and depend on a car. In addition, tourists must also be able to comfortably visit our city by car. Burgerbelang therefore advocates better signage for the available parking spaces.

261827.explanation: There is a shortage of affordable homes to buy and rental homes above the rent subsidy limit for middle incomes. We also need to build for the elderly. For now, 30% is sufficient for Burgerbelang.

261828.explanation: There are currently enough car-free streets.

261829.explanation: This should be possible under conditions—especially for 'older' entrepreneurs—because we gain nothing if these businesses lose their company and end up on social assistance.

261830.explanation: Mainly the rental of mandatory components through the municipality (warning signs, crowd barriers) is very high. These prices should certainly be reduced. However, Burgerbelang also advocates for more parties and festivals in the neighborhoods.

261831.explanation: The speed in residential streets should be lowered. This is already the case in many streets and only brings benefits.

261832.explanation: Extensive participation and surveys are better than a one-sided yes/no decision through a referendum.

261833.explanation: The idea is nice, but in many municipalities it does not work as intended: namely to involve young people more. It is also challenging for the child concerned to participate. An active youth council works better.

261834.explanation: That helps to bring the council closer to the people, provided there is also space created for local residents. In that case, it can also be held multiple times a year.

261836.explanation: The municipality can certainly stimulate starting entrepreneurs, but waiving property tax under specific conditions is complex and unfeasible. Targeted subsidies and exemptions work much better.

261837.explanation: Where possible, Burgerbelang wants to stimulate the local economy. We want to interpret 'local' more broadly, namely awarding contracts to companies from our region.

261838.explanation: All permit applications must be processed quickly and efficiently.

261839.explanation: 'The polluter pays' is a good principle. Burgerbelang always strives for affordable waste services for everyone.

261840.explanation: We agree with this statement because thanks to Burgerbelang there has been a cozy and successful terrace policy for years; terraces may now stay open until 1 a.m. We are happy with this extension. Further extensions would jeopardize livability and safety.

261841.explanation: As a municipality, we already do more than required by the Dispersion Act. Therefore, Burgerbelang believes we should stick to this. The surrounding municipalities should now also take responsibility.

261842.explanation: As a municipality, we already do more than required. Therefore, Burgerbelang believes we should stick to this.

261843.explanation: The municipality has an effort obligation from the national government. We comply with this. We especially want to look at a fairer allocation system for young people and others who have been on the waiting list for a long time.

261844.explanation: Many people enjoy fireworks. Fireworks are also an important part of the success of the fair. Burgerbelang believes that fireworks displays by a company or municipality should remain possible.

261846.explanation: As soon as batteries can actually store a lot of energy, this could be an option. For now, much remains unclear.

261847.explanation: For Burgerbelang, neighborliness is very important, and then you must be able to meet each other, including in the evenings.

261848.explanation: People pay extra to have the container emptied, so the polluter pays. That's a fine arrangement and convenient for those with mobility issues. The containers also encourage people to separate waste more consciously.

261849.explanation: The municipality has an obligation to meet conditions concerning the emission of nitrogen and pesticides in the future. Agricultural activities within the municipality are very limited and must be assessed on a case-by-case basis.

261850.explanation: The IJsselslag must definitely be made more attractive for more target groups. Additionally, Burgerbelang advocates more recreational opportunities at the Bronsbergen open water.

261851.explanation: Currently, Zutphen has at most one Easter fire within the municipal boundaries. This tradition is manageable. We will be more critical of new applications.

261852.explanation: The municipality already takes biodiversity into account in the design. Additionally, there are already enough rules for nature. Extra rules hinder housing construction. The layout of a neighborhood is also more up to the residents in consultation with the municipality.

261853.explanation: Burgerbelang believes that things should not deteriorate further. Finances must be tightly controlled.

261854.explanation: A youth center for young people under 18 is highly desirable. Young people are being driven from place to place. They must be able to be under one roof in a pleasant and safe way.

261855.explanation: Associations already receive subsidies through rental contracts. Linking contributions to rent is unfair, with some associations benefiting more than others. Through the introduction of a Zutphen pass, we can individually assess what people need.

261856.explanation: Burgerbelang would like to see a school, both vocational (MBO) and higher professional education (HBO). There are many opportunities for technical and care education in the municipality of Zutphen.

261857.explanation: Burgerbelang has campaigned for years to combat low literacy and it is important to continue this effort.

261858.explanation: Illegal dumping has long been an eyesore, especially for residents of the Waterkwartier. We advocate for stricter enforcement and, if possible, camera surveillance. Additionally, we want to make it easier for neighborhood residents to dispose of bulky waste a few times a year.

261859.explanation: Given the housing shortage and limited space within our municipality, this is a very good location.

261860.explanation: The municipality currently has broadly supported and active housing corporations. A housing company would add little to this. A housing development company focused on stimulating housing construction for everyone, both owner-occupied and rental, would be better.

261861.explanation: Zutphen must counteract vacancy and speed up housing projects. There can be many reasons for a house being vacant and a tailored approach is needed. There are already rules and resources for this.

261863.explanation: Older people often want to move, but there are no suitable homes. Also, for many tenants, higher rents are a barrier. The national government, housing corporations, and municipality should handle this differently.

261864.explanation: If there is demand for this type of 'kangaroo homes' (multigenerational housing), then this is certainly desirable. We want to build according to need.

261865.explanation: This seems like a good approach in the municipality. We warmly support the development of co-housing projects such as a Knarrenhof in Warnsveld.

261866.explanation: Burgerbelang does not want to realize high-rise buildings in the city center but, given the housing shortage, wants to be flexible about building heights in the surrounding area with a maximum of 12 floors, provided it fits well and is desirable.

261867.explanation: There are already various agencies for that.

261868.explanation: We are committed to quickly supporting people in the municipality with problematic debts. A pilot or trial project where the debt is taken over can help. Clear agreements must be made about this.

261869.explanation: Managing money wisely for all residents is an important educational topic for a municipality. However, there are already many initiatives in society to support refugees in this area. A broad information campaign would be more effective.

261871.explanation: Parents are free to provide their toddlers with the nutrition the child needs. For those with less financial means, there are already several (municipal) schemes to help with costs.

261872.explanation: Burgerbelang finds car accessibility of the city center very important. Therefore, removing above-ground parking can only happen if there is compensation via a parking garage near the center.

261873.explanation: The 's Gravenhof can only be made car-free if there are sufficient (underground) parking facilities in the immediate vicinity.

261874.explanation: Citizens' Interest is always open to improving the welfare of vulnerable groups. Whether the municipality is the appropriate body to initiate such a project is not yet clear. Health insurers should likely play a better role here.

261876.explanation: Given the drug problems and harmful effects of social media, there is certainly a need for more youth assistance in Zutphen.

261816.explanation: Farms should be given the space to engage in business activities such as a shop or campsite. The municipality must assess per location whether this fits with the surroundings, nature, livability regarding traffic, fair competition, and the environment.

261817.explanation: Wood burning can be harmful to health. In unsuitable weather, the municipality may intervene to keep the air cleaner.

261818.explanation: Sport is good for health, self-confidence, and social participation. Money should never be a reason to be unable to play sports. This prevents exclusion and we invest in prevention.

261820.explanation: More enforcers increase visibility and safety in neighborhoods where there is nuisance.

261821.explanation: A permanent neighborhood police officer builds trust and prevents problems. It is very important that the police are again easily approachable in the neighborhood.

261822.explanation: D66 is cautious about camera surveillance due to privacy concerns. In Schupstoel, other measures against serious nuisance have not been sufficiently effective yet. Temporary and targeted camera surveillance can help, with clear objectives and regular evaluation.

261823.explanation: Dangerous behavior on fatbikes must be addressed to protect others. It is crucial that traffic safety is given high priority.

261824.explanation: There is a high demand for social rental housing in Zutphen. By giving priority to people with a clear connection to Zutphen, starters, families with children, and the elderly can continue living in their own municipality.

261825.explanation: Enforcement officers should resolve problems calmly through authority and dialogue. A baton does not fit this role. Enforcement officers are supervisors, not police.

261826.explanation: The city center is primarily intended for walking, cycling, and staying. Cars remain welcome but are not leading. Good accessibility is important for entrepreneurs and a lively city center. Therefore, we focus on underground parking garages.

261827.explanation: There are too few affordable homes and waiting times are long. Social housing in all neighborhoods is urgently needed.

261828.explanation: Fewer cars make streets safer, livelier, and more pleasant to use.

261829.explanation: Entrepreneurs in financial trouble should be able to get help. Good debt assistance can prevent them from losing their business and increases the chances of a successful restart.

261830.explanation: We believe it is important that parties and festivals take place not only in the city center but also in Warnsveld and all neighborhoods. Excessive costs should not prevent this.

261831.explanation: For traffic safety and livability in neighborhoods, 30 km/h zones are important. This enables children from age 8 to cycle safely and independently to school. Slower traffic makes streets calmer and gives priority to walking, cycling, city buses, and public transport.

261832.explanation: A referendum can be a good tool if residents are well informed neutrally and in advance it is clear what the result means. A merger has major consequences, so residents must be able to participate in the discussion.

261833.explanation: Children learn at a young age how democracy works.

261834.explanation: Holding meetings in the neighborhood makes the municipal council more visible and easier to reach. This can happen more often than once a year. Also, the Speakers' Square for public speakers can be held in a different neighborhood each time.

261836.explanation: Starters deserve support, provided it is temporary and clearly defined.

261837.explanation: Where possible, it is good to involve local businesses. However, legal regulations remain in force.

261838.explanation: Permits must be handled fairly and carefully. All permit applications should be processed faster; it currently takes too long for both private individuals and entrepreneurs to receive a permit.

261839.explanation: The waste pile must be reduced. Those who produce more waste must pay more. This encourages less waste.

261840.explanation: D66 opposes extending terrace opening hours because it harms livability and the nighttime rest of residents. The hospitality industry may remain open inside in the evenings; public spaces are not an extension of nighttime hospitality.

261841.explanation: It has been agreed with the residents of Zutphen that no more than a maximum of 740 asylum seekers will be housed. D66 stands for a reliable government.

261842.explanation: D66 is not automatically for or against. If it is necessary to make our sports hall or other facilities available for crisis shelter, we support that.

261843.explanation: Municipalities must give priority to status holders with residence permits to fulfill the legal task. As long as this task exists, that priority is necessary.

261844.explanation: Less fireworks results in less nuisance and less pollution of the Natura 2000 area, which we must protect.

261846.explanation: Sustainable energy is necessary for the future. We want to invest in accelerating the energy transition.

261847.explanation: Community centers provide meeting and connection opportunities. Therefore, it is important that they are open not only in the evenings but also on weekends. This offers people more chances for contact and helps reduce loneliness.

261848.explanation: Waste policy must be clear and feasible for residents. If people want to choose a gray bin, that should remain possible.

261849.explanation: Organic farming is better for nature and the environment.

261850.explanation: A swimming pool is important for sports and meeting people. Expansion requires careful consideration.

261851.explanation: Easter fires are harmful to the environment, people, and animals.

261852.explanation: D66 supports nature-inclusive building. This helps nature and creates healthier neighborhoods that are more resilient to heat and water.

261853.explanation: Culture deserves a strong and good place in the city. The Hanzehuis is important for Zutphen. Culture brings life to the city center and strengthens the local economy.

261854.explanation: Young people need safe places to meet each other. This is important for their mental health, relaxation, and development. Because young people are diverse, D66 does not advocate a single central youth center but prefers small-scale facilities in the neighborhoods.

261855.explanation: Sport must remain affordable for everyone, including those on low incomes. Higher costs make sports increasingly difficult. Sport keeps people healthy and prevents healthcare costs. Therefore, the municipality invests in sports with agreements that are also feasible for sports clubs.

261856.explanation: Higher education offers opportunities for young people and strengthens the city. We would like to see higher professional education (HBO) in Zutphen.

261857.explanation: The language deficit in Zutphen is higher than the national average. Extra language lessons help children with a language delay. Equal opportunities mean that we invest extra where needed to prevent disadvantages as early as possible.

261858.explanation: Garbage placed next to containers causes mess and nuisance and must be addressed.

261859.explanation: Zutphen has little room for expansion, but this location is suitable. Due to the housing shortage, new living spaces are needed, with respect for nature.

261860.explanation: Housing corporations are not profit-driven. They already work with clear performance agreements with the municipality.

261861.explanation: Vacancy is unacceptable in times of housing shortage. This measure can help reduce the housing crisis. It encourages owners to make homes available on the housing market more quickly and helps shorten housing shortages and waiting times.

261863.explanation: Moving helps elderly people find suitable housing and ensures flow-through. This frees up larger homes for other families. Many elderly find moving difficult due to all that it entails. Therefore, support and financial help are important.

261864.explanation: Homes suitable for grandparents living in make cohabitation and care easier. There is a great need for varied forms of housing.

261865.explanation: Building together makes housing more affordable and strengthens involvement. D66 supports varied forms of housing.

261866.explanation: A maximum building height of 15 meters applies in the city center. We want to maintain this.

261867.explanation: Tenants must be protected against abuses. Due to the Affordable Rent Act, the municipality is required to help tenants facing excessively high rent.

261868.explanation: This can prevent a lot of stress and problems for individuals and families with debts. Having one debt with the municipality provides peace and overview and helps restore financial self-reliance.

261869.explanation: D66 supports this because it helps prevent debts. Learning to manage money gives people equal opportunities and helps them participate actively. Financial knowledge prevents problems.

261871.explanation: A healthy start is important for young children. With a vegetable bag, parents learn early on about healthy eating for their baby. This helps with good development and works preventively for health.

261872.explanation: Underground parking can free up surface space for greenery. Especially in Zutphen, where space is so scarce, we will need to move towards underground parking.

261873.explanation: 's Gravenhof is intended as a place to stay and meet each other. In our vision, ’s Gravenhof will become a green and attractive square in the museum district of the city.

261874.explanation: For some groups of children, dental care is a major problem. With preventive care, we reach children in time. Early help prevents bigger problems later.

261876.explanation: Early support helps young people better. It is very important that low-threshold youth workers are available in youth centers to support young people.

261816.explanation: The VVD stands for freedom of entrepreneurship.

261817.explanation: People are well able to resolve nuisance issues among themselves. A ban that is impossible to enforce concerning what happens behind the front door is a symbolic measure.

261818.explanation: Everyone must be able to play sports. The municipality must ensure that people have sufficient income. Fortunately, schemes like the Participation scheme already exist to support people in this.

261820.explanation: The VVD stands for a safe municipality. This requires sufficient enforcement officers.

261821.explanation: Neighborhood officers must engage in conversations with residents; they already do this every month. The VVD believes it is up to the police themselves to organize their tasks and set their priorities.

261822.explanation: Camera surveillance increases the chance of catching offenders and improves the feeling of safety. The VVD wants to apply camera surveillance in multiple locations in the municipality.

261823.explanation: The VVD believes the municipality must tackle all forms of traffic nuisance. Reducing nuisance caused by fatbikes is certainly part of this.

261824.explanation: The VVD believes it is important that people can quickly find a (rental) home in their own municipality.

261825.explanation: The VVD wants to use camera surveillance and bodycams to increase safety. A baton could in the future become part of the equipment to perform tasks.

261826.explanation: You should be able to decide for yourself how to get from A to B. An accessible city center is very important for both residents and entrepreneurs.

261827.explanation: The VVD wants to maintain the current percentage and put more focus on flow-through.

261828.explanation: The city center must remain livable and accessible.

261829.explanation: Helping entrepreneurs with debts already exists; the VVD wants to maintain this.

261830.explanation: The municipality's fees must be reduced in any case.

261831.explanation: There are streets where roads can and must be adjusted. The VVD believes the priority should be enforcement.

261832.explanation: The municipality must consult with residents and weigh their opinions heavily. The elected representatives exist to make the final decision.

261833.explanation: A children's mayor contributes to involving young people in politics and ensures that young people are heard.

261834.explanation: Meeting at various locations is confusing and a waste of taxpayers' money. The town hall belongs to everyone.

261836.explanation: Starting entrepreneurs create jobs and take risks. They deserve a helping hand.

261837.explanation: By purchasing locally, we stimulate local entrepreneurs.

261838.explanation: Permits must be granted faster in any case. The VVD wants to cut unnecessary rules and speed up the permit process for everyone.

261839.explanation: The polluter pays. People who separate their waste properly should not be penalized.

261840.explanation: Longer opening hours make terraces and cafes more attractive to young people.

261841.explanation: With the existing asylum seekers' center and the shelter for Ukrainians, Zutphen already more than meets the standards and societal responsibility.

261842.explanation: Zutphen is already doing enough. As far as the VVD is concerned: no extra location, no community center.

261843.explanation: Zutphen must meet the quota; giving priority is unfair and unnecessary.

261844.explanation: Fireworks and the fair go together. This tradition should be honored.

261846.explanation: Neighborhood batteries are not a municipal responsibility. If people want to arrange this themselves, the municipality should be helpful.

261847.explanation: Many community centers are already open in the evenings. Community centers can perfectly decide for themselves when they are open.

261848.explanation: For those who want to, the grey bin will remain. This way, people can decide how they dispose of their waste.

261849.explanation: Land should be leased to the party with the best plan.

261850.explanation: The municipality must contribute to a future-proof swimming pool; this can be done in multiple ways.

261851.explanation: The municipality should not interfere with traditions like Easter fires that take place properly and safely. The municipality has other priorities.

261852.explanation: The VVD stands for green neighborhoods, more homes, and more nature near homes. More regulations are not the way to increase greenery; rules do not plant extra trees.

261853.explanation: The VVD is critical of the high costs of the Hanzehuis and monitors expenses very closely. However, it is important to realize a future-proof theater.

261854.explanation: Youth do not limit themselves to one place; a single youth center does not do justice to our youth.

261855.explanation: The VVD values our community life greatly. The municipality must support associations; determining membership fees is up to the associations themselves.

261856.explanation: An HBO (higher professional education) institution and expansion of MBO (secondary vocational education) programs bring many benefits to entrepreneurs and retain more young people in our municipality.

261857.explanation: The municipality must comply with all legal rules and use all the existing incentive programs.

261858.explanation: The polluter pays. The VVD wants to use camera surveillance and more police presence on the streets to increase the chance of catching offenders and impose stricter punishments. We stand for a clean municipality.

261859.explanation: Because the homes are no longer used for holidays but increasingly for living, Bronsbergen is already a residential area. The VVD does believe the municipality should not bear the costs if Bronsbergen becomes a residential area.

261860.explanation: This is a task for housing corporations and private individuals. A municipality reinventing the wheel only causes more delays in building homes.

261861.explanation: Empty buildings are a waste; therefore, the municipality must look for ways to prevent vacancy. However, people must be free to decide what to do with their property.

261863.explanation: The problem lies in the shortage of life course-resilient homes. A premium to push people out of their houses would be better invested in building these homes.

261864.explanation: Building should be based on needs. Those who want to realize a caregiver’s home should be able to do so more easily.

261865.explanation: The municipality should not designate locations itself, but if an initiative arises, the municipality should respond positively.

261866.explanation: There are already apartment buildings taller than eight floors. The VVD is critical about the location, taking into account, for example, the village character of Warnsveld and De Hoven.

261867.explanation: The Rent Tribunal already exists. Creating an additional body is unnecessary.

261868.explanation: This is already existing policy. With one creditor there is more oversight. The VVD is critical of recurring debts and wants to focus more on prevention.

261869.explanation: This task belongs to schools and organizations that help refugees integrate.

261871.explanation: The municipality must ensure that households have sufficient disposable income instead of setting up numerous temporary schemes.

261872.explanation: The VVD stands for an accessible city center. If an underground parking garage is needed for that, then that should definitely be possible.

261873.explanation: No good alternative yet exists for the current parking spaces. Without a good nearby alternative, parking spaces cannot yet be removed. The city center must remain easily accessible.

261874.explanation: Dental care is not a task for the municipality.

261876.explanation: The municipality must work together with schools and youth care to focus more on prevention, counteract bullying among other things, and build resilience in young people. More youth workers are not the solution.

261816.explanation: Small farms are struggling because they have to produce more and more to survive. At the same time, they can provide affordable fun by opening a small campsite. This way, affordable recreation in our countryside remains accessible to everyone.

261817.explanation: We oppose moralizing interference from the municipality about how people should live their lives. We also oppose unnecessary and unhealthy fine particulate matter in the air. There are easy ways to prevent nuisance in the neighborhood without banning wood burning, such as using good wood and the correct burning technique.

261818.explanation: Sporting together brings people together and must be affordable for everyone. This plan ensures no one is left out. The best solution is to lower membership fees for (sports) clubs for everyone. This is possible if the municipality charges lower rent costs from the clubs.

261820.explanation: Safety in our streets is important. BOAs (special enforcement officers) are taking over more and more work from the national police. The police can do less and less due to drastic cutbacks. A safe municipality also needs a strong public sector: good education, green maintenance, and social facilities.

261821.explanation: Neighborhood police officers know their own neighborhood best and are the first point of contact for residents. Neighborhood officers can offer a listening ear and help solve local problems before they get worse. Visibility of the neighborhood officer also contributes to a feeling of safety.

261822.explanation: Camera surveillance infringes on people's privacy and provides false security. Moreover, camera surveillance may only be used if other means have failed. We have a right to privacy in public spaces. Also at the Schupstoel.

261823.explanation: Our municipality should be a safe place. Anti-social and dangerous behavior on any mode of transport—fatbike or not—is not normal. It is the responsibility of all of us to address each other about this. Where useful and effective, the police can conduct extra checks.

261824.explanation: Thousands of people rooted in our municipality, who play sports and work here, cannot find housing. They have first right to their own place, in their own social environment. The best solution is to build more social rental homes so that everyone who wants to can find a place here.

261825.explanation: Shortages in the police force should not be a reason to deploy enforcement officers (BOAs) for tasks they are not trained for. The use of force is a last resort and belongs to the police.

261826.explanation: Our city must be accessible to everyone. People with mobility issues often depend on a car. A reachable city is also an affordable city. If parking costs continue to rise, parking in the city center will only be possible for the very wealthy.

261827.explanation: There have been enough expensive houses built. Project developers and land speculators have made huge profits from them. It is high time to choose more affordable social rental housing. The municipality can start today by converting the vacant Baudartius building into social rental housing.

261828.explanation: Our shopping streets are already car-free. People who live in the city center also have the right to a place to park their car nearby without huge parking fees.

261829.explanation: Every entrepreneur should - like every other resident - be able to apply for debt counseling. Certain conditions also apply for entrepreneurs. The SP also believes the municipality should stop involving commercial administrators who profit from other people's debts.

261830.explanation: Parties and festivals are part of our shared culture. By lowering the fee for a permit, we ensure that these events can take place more easily. Tickets and drinks can thereby be cheaper so that they remain accessible to everyone.

261831.explanation: Residents in neighborhoods should be able to decide for themselves about lowering the maximum speed limit in their area. For traffic safety, it is more important to tackle the reckless drivers who drive well above 50 where only 50 is allowed.

261832.explanation: A merger with another municipality affects the identity of our community. Inhabitants should be asked for their opinion on this. Not only the views of administrators and politicians count. Residents experience the consequences in daily life.

261833.explanation: We see no added value in a child mayor. Children should mainly be free and relaxed to play, to fall, and to make mistakes. The symbolic role of child mayor places too much pressure on a child who is still growing up.

261834.explanation: The Zutphen representatives should be rooted in the community, visible and among the people. You can rely on SP council members for this, but many other council members are mainly good at meetings and do not hear what lives in the community. Therefore, it is good to bring them to all neighborhoods.

261836.explanation: Everyone in our municipality pays property tax according to their means. With the money collected, we keep our streets clean and provide important public facilities that entrepreneurs also benefit from. We also ask new residents to pay taxes during their first three years.

261837.explanation: Municipal projects are often tendered to companies that offer the lowest bid. Usually, these companies are not from Zutphen, with all the resulting consequences. It is good for our own economy if the municipality played a more important role in construction projects and who executes them.

261838.explanation: Every permit application should be processed quickly. Applicants need to know their position quickly, and no complicated priority rules are necessary for that.

261839.explanation: People with a lot of waste should not be fined. Keeping our municipality clean is a joint responsibility. We pay the bill together through an annual fixed amount. This also prevents waste dumping, which is currently happening a lot.

261840.explanation: A lively city center is a fun city center. That is what the city center is for.

261841.explanation: Zutphen is already a caring municipality for people who need care and shelter, such as refugee reception and mental health care. We do three times as much as we are required to. Wealthy municipalities that have neglected their duty for years should now take their share of the reception.

261842.explanation: The shelter crisis exists because national procedures take too long, and there are too few homes for status holders allowed to stay here. Temporary shelter is a superficial solution. It is more important that other municipalities also house their status holders, as Zutphen already does properly.

261843.explanation: The municipality must ensure that no one needs priority and no one has to jump the queue. This is possible if enough social rental homes are built. At least 50% in all new construction projects. Then you tackle the problem at the root.

261844.explanation: The funfair with a large fireworks show is a deeply felt tradition for many residents. Traditions are very important to a community. Stopping this show would also mean the end of the funfair because the evening with the fireworks show is the most popular of all.

261846.explanation: The SP believes that greening can be better organized collectively and should not be the responsibility of individuals. With a neighborhood battery, we can ensure that all generated energy remains available for a neighborhood or district. That saves us all a lot of money, which we see back on the energy bill.

261847.explanation: Community centers are an important place where people can meet each other. Every neighborhood should have a community center. Opening them in the evenings contributes to their accessibility. Ultimately, the neighborhood itself should have control over their social spaces.

261848.explanation: The municipality must ensure that residents can dispose of their waste easily and properly. The grey bin collected at home is very important and indispensable for this. Eliminating it makes it harder for residents and increases the chance of littering. We all suffer from that.

261849.explanation: Industrial farming driven by the agro-industry is a major cause of exploitation of our land: damage to our environment, biodiversity, and nitrogen emissions. According to the SP, the municipality has an important role in this greening by promoting organic farming.

261850.explanation: It was a blunder that the municipality demolished the outdated Graaf Ottobad and only rebuilt a competition pool. The SP has always warned about this. The IJsselslag must still get a slide so families no longer have to travel to swimming pools in other municipalities.

261851.explanation: A bonfire at Easter is an age-old tradition and an essential part of our folk identity in the Achterhoek. It increases togetherness and social cohesion. Additionally, we believe people should decide themselves about the future and shape of traditions. That is not the role of politics.

261852.explanation: It is important for the hundreds of residents in our municipality who have been waiting for a home for years that sufficient (affordable) housing is built as soon as possible. Fortunately, there are already many rules in place to protect nature. More rules would only delay construction.

261853.explanation: The Hanzehuis is a prestige project to show off to neighboring municipalities. Our city does not need a cultural palace costing over 30 million. That money would be better spent ensuring affordable housing, for example in the Baudartius building and other vacant buildings.

261854.explanation: Young people have been saying for decades that they lack their own meeting place. There is a connection between boredom and juvenile delinquency. A youth center can be an important part of youth policy, which also includes education, youth care, Safe Home, and youth work.

261855.explanation: Playing sports together strengthens community spirit and must be affordable and accessible to everyone. To achieve this, sports clubs need to lower membership fees for everyone. This is possible if the municipality reduces rental costs for sports fields and gyms or subsidizes clubs.

261856.explanation: Zutphen has good primary and secondary education, but the vocational education offer is meager. HBO (higher professional education) would be a good addition. However, we do believe that education funds should go to education and not campaigns.

261857.explanation: The municipality must invest to counter delays among babies, toddlers, preschoolers, and youths. The SP finds it important for the development of our children that parents can receive much more support and guidance from the consultative clinic than is currently the case.

261858.explanation: The mess around waste containers is the result of poor waste policy. Removing the grey bin only worsens this. The municipality must ensure that everyone can dispose of their waste easily and properly instead of punishing the consequences of poor policy.

261859.explanation: Recreation park Bronsbergen has been permanently inhabited from day one. Because of a naive municipal government, the project developer has been able to cash in heavily. If new houses are built, they must be affordable homes for Zutphen residents, not for recreation or wealthy westerners.

261860.explanation: To be able to build truly affordable homes, a local non-profit housing company is needed, owned by all of us. Then rents will not have to rise sharply every year, as housing corporations and slumlords currently do. The housing crisis will never be solved with this current weak policy.

261861.explanation: Many houses in Zutphen also stand empty while so many people are looking for a home. Property owners often make more profit from speculation on vacancy than from renting out. This is unfair and must be tackled with a vacancy fine.

261863.explanation: Older people decide for themselves, with their own considerations, whether to leave their home. When they do, it is a big step and creates new housing for all those looking. A sum of money can be very welcome to arrange the move properly and for a good start in the new home.

261864.explanation: If grandparents cannot or do not want to live independently, the SP considers care community homes the best solution. But homes suitable for multiple generations are also needed, of course.

261865.explanation: Cooperative building is a step in the right direction, but it is not feasible for all residents. Often it concerns a small group with enough money. The SP wants to go a step further and wants the municipality to build itself.

261866.explanation: There is a great need for housing, so as much (and as tall as possible) must be built. The height of apartment blocks should primarily be determined by whether it fits the surroundings and neighborhood. Think, for example, of the Sterflat with 12 floors that has been there for years.

261867.explanation: Rental houses often have many more problems besides high rents, such as mold, poor insulation, and drafts. It is important that a rental team is personally reachable at the municipality to help solve these problems. Everyone benefits from well-maintained and affordable housing.

261868.explanation: This arrangement provides much peace and clarity for people with debts. It also makes it possible to receive good assistance from the municipality. In this way, we stop outsourcing this help to companies that want to profit from debts.

261869.explanation: If status holders do not yet have housing and live in a reception center, the COA is responsible for this. If status holders live in our municipality, they can make use of existing facilities that apply to everyone.

261871.explanation: A good healthy start in life is extremely valuable for development and opportunities later in life. The initiative for a free vegetable bag is a way in which the municipality can truly make a difference in people's lives.

261872.explanation: The SP is not against underground parking garages. But it is important to carefully weigh the benefits and for whom they are intended. Moreover, they cost a lot of money.

261873.explanation: Our city center must remain accessible to everyone. People with limited mobility are driven out of the city due to insufficient parking spaces.

261874.explanation: A healthy set of teeth is important for children. Every child deserves good dental care, regardless of the parents’ financial situation.

261876.explanation: Youth workers should be the central figures when it comes to guiding young people outside the family. Every school in Zutphen should be connected to a youth worker. This youth worker has contacts with youth care, neighborhood police officers, teachers, and parents.

261816.explanation: We must help farmers transition to organic farming. If this includes a campsite or shop, that is fine. But if it contributes to maintaining the factory farming system, the Party for the Animals is against it.

261817.explanation: Everyone has the right to clean air. Wood burning causes a lot of air pollution and worsens health complaints. A ban is necessary in unsuitable weather conditions.

261818.explanation: Sports are important for health and social connection. Lack of money should not be a barrier to participating in society. We want to make sports accessible to those who currently participate little, such as the elderly, refugees, and children from low-income families.

261820.explanation: BOAs (community enforcement officers) do important work. We want Zutphen to also invest in ears and eyes in the neighborhood: neighborhood parents, neighborhood coaches, and a dedicated police officer for each district. This helps prevent crime.

261821.explanation: Fixed contact persons for the neighborhood, such as neighborhood coaches and community police officers, and low-threshold access to the police are important to address problems early. Therefore, we support a weekly consultation hour.

261822.explanation: Camera surveillance infringes on privacy and mainly offers a false sense of security. We prefer prevention, social presence, and well-designed public spaces.

261823.explanation: We want the police to better monitor traffic safety in general.

261824.explanation: Housing is a human right. We want fair allocation without exclusion, with attention to people in vulnerable situations. It is also legally prohibited to give priority to people from one’s own municipality.

261825.explanation: BOAs are there for supervision and prevention. De-escalation and trust fit their role better than further arming. There are no signs in Zutphen indicating that this is necessary.

261826.explanation: The city center must be livable, green, and safe. Pedestrians, cyclists, and public transport have priority over cars.

261827.explanation: Affordable housing is a basic right. More social rental housing is necessary to fairly address the housing crisis. We support at least 40% social rental units in new construction.

261828.explanation: Fewer cars and more greenery make the city center safer and more attractive. Children can play on the streets again. It is better for nature, animals, and the climate, and more pleasant for visitors, which also strengthens the local economy. Consider Beukerstraat, which has been successfully car-free for years.

261829.explanation: By tackling problems early, we prevent bankruptcies and personal suffering. That is socially and economically wise. We consider this a good idea for viable businesses.

261830.explanation: Costs must be fair. Sustainable and animal-friendly events can be rewarded; polluting ones cannot. The principle is that permits for commercial activities should be cost-covering.

261831.explanation: Lower speeds result in fewer accidents, less noise, and a safer environment for people and animals.

261832.explanation: Such a significant decision requires direct input from residents. We support advisory referenda for important decisions.

261833.explanation: Children deserve a voice. This strengthens engagement and democratic awareness at a young age.

261834.explanation: Politics must be visible and accessible, close to residents. Additionally, we want to make it attractive to attend council meetings.

261836.explanation: Both residents and businesses contribute via taxes to our society. Exempting starters from property tax causes unfair competition. The Party for the Animals stands for a level playing field. This is also not legally possible.

261837.explanation: This strengthens the local economy and results in less transport and emissions. This keeps money in the region and makes us more sustainable. In some cases, this is legally not possible.

261838.explanation: Permits must be assessed carefully and fairly, with attention to nature, animals, and neighbors.

261839.explanation: The polluter pays. Those who produce less waste and separate better must also pay less.

261840.explanation: Liveliness is important, but not at the expense of peace, health of neighbors, and animals. Zutphen already offers generous opening hours for terraces.

261841.explanation: Other municipalities often do not take sufficient responsibility. Therefore, Zutphen should not rigidly limit itself to a maximum of 740, but continue to provide humane shelter for people fleeing hardship, based on compassion and solidarity.

261842.explanation: Precisely because other municipalities fall short, Zutphen demonstrates that solidarity and humane shelter are important. The municipality continues to take responsibility out of compassion.

261843.explanation: A home is essential for a safe start and good integration. Housing is a basic right. Refugees cannot register as housing seekers in advance and otherwise fall behind in the queue.

261844.explanation: The fireworks show is set off in or near a Natura 2000 area and causes damage to animals, nature, and air quality. A fair can also be attractive and profitable with sustainable alternatives, such as a party tent or light show.

261846.explanation: Neighborhood batteries make it possible to store and use locally generated energy together. This strengthens the energy transition, relieves the grid, and makes Zutphen less dependent on energy from other countries. Moreover, residents share the benefits.

261847.explanation: Community centers are important for meeting, social cohesion, and well-being. Residents should also be able to go there in the evening to meet each other and organize activities.

261848.explanation: Waste policy should mainly focus on less waste and better separation. The system must help residents reduce waste. We are curious about the results of the pilot conducted on this.

261849.explanation: In this way, the municipality stimulates nature-friendly and sustainable farming. This is better for the soil, water, biodiversity, and animal welfare.

261850.explanation: Sports and physical activity are healthy, preventive, and have an important social function. An attractive swimming pool invites young and old to exercise and meet each other. For expansion, we choose sustainable water purification, such as salt or natural purification instead of chlorine.

261851.explanation: Easter fires cause a lot of smoke and harmful air pollution, especially in spring when many animals are active or have young. Hedgehogs and other animals shelter in woodpiles, where they burn alive. As understandable as the tradition is, animal suffering and health damage weigh more heavily.

261852.explanation: By building nature-inclusive with green roofs and façades, nesting boxes, and hanging gardens, we help birds, bats, insects, and hedgehogs. This strengthens biodiversity, reduces heat stress, and helps combat climate change.

261853.explanation: Culture connects, inspires, and offers space for meeting, education, and creativity. The Hanzehuis can accommodate performing arts, social activities, and creators. For the Party for the Animals, it is essential that this building is designed to be nature-inclusive.

261854.explanation: Young people need safe places to meet each other, be themselves, and develop. A youth center contributes to well-being, connection, and prevention.

261855.explanation: Sports must be accessible to everyone. By charging clubs lower rent in exchange for lower membership fees, more people can participate, promoting health and social connection.

261856.explanation: Zutphen is aging. To remain attractive and vibrant, we must offer something to young people. An institution of higher professional education or branch, for example in care, welfare or anthroposophy, fits Zutphen. More young people also strengthen association life and make culture and nightlife viable in Zutphen and Warnsveld.

261857.explanation: A good start in language prevents delays that are difficult to catch up on later. Toddler daycare with additional language offerings helps children communicate, play, and learn better together. Problems can also be identified early so that children receive the right support in time for a good start at school.

261858.explanation: However, the municipality must ensure that the containers are emptied on time and are functional. Leaving waste next to the containers causes litter and nuisance.

261859.explanation: Bronsbergen is a valuable, green, and water-rich area. We believe other locations are more suitable for housing. Housing does not have to come at the expense of nature and landscape.

261860.explanation: Housing construction is often now driven by profit, so money determines who can live there and profits flow to large companies. Housing is a right, not a business model. With a municipal housing company, Zutphen will have more control over affordable housing and the money will benefit society.

261861.explanation: Vacancy is unacceptable in times of housing shortage. Homes are meant to be lived in, not left empty or speculated on.

261863.explanation: We do not want relocation allowances. But we do want to offer help in finding a suitable home. This creates space for families and leads to a balanced composition of neighborhoods.

261864.explanation: This proposal results in more larger houses being built. We prefer to build more homes. At the same time, informal care homes offer more flexibility. This solution provides an option for a larger group of people.

261865.explanation: There is a need for affordable housing in Zutphen. Such homes are more often in the expensive segment. Space where houses can be built is scarce in Zutphen.

261866.explanation: For the Party for the Animals a fixed height is not decisive, but what is necessary for good and sustainable housing construction. By occasionally building higher, we can realize more homes on the same land area with more greenery.

261867.explanation: Unfortunately, abuses occur in the rental sector. Many tenants do not know how to address this, causing stress and uncertainty. A tenant team offers low-threshold assistance and increases well-being. The municipality of Zutphen must be there for everyone and stand for a just society.

261868.explanation: People can get into debt through no fault of their own, for example due to illness or divorce. Debt causes a lot of stress and uncertainty. Out of compassion and concern for residents’ well-being, the municipality must quickly provide calm and perspective, especially now that debt relief too often is a business model.

261869.explanation: The municipality must help people who have difficulty managing money. It does not matter whether you are a refugee or not. Everyone should receive appropriate help.

261871.explanation: Healthy nutrition at a young age is essential. By supporting parents with vegetables and instructions on preparation, children learn to eat vegetables early. What they learn young, they often maintain for life. A plant-based lifestyle is also the future: good for people, animals, and the climate.

261872.explanation: A parking garage is only possible if it does not come at the expense of nature and replaces existing parking spaces. This creates space for greening and a low-car, clean, and safe city. Parking on the edges is logical; existing parking spaces must first be made more visible and better used.

261873.explanation: By making the ’s Gravenhof car-free, space is created for greenery, clean air, and biodiversity. The square becomes safer and more attractive to stay, with room for meeting and play. This is good for people, animals, and the climate. The inner city then becomes even more attractive to tourists.

261874.explanation: Good oral care prevents many other health problems and medical costs. Poor teeth can cause pain, embarrassment, and social exclusion. With a dental bus we make dental care accessible so every child can grow up healthy and participate fully.

261876.explanation: More and more young people suffer from stress and mental problems. Youth workers are low-threshold and close by. Early support prevents more severe problems later and increases well-being and resilience.

261816.explanation: Kies Bewust Lokaal encourages entrepreneurship. Make permit applications easy so entrepreneurs can start and grow faster. We sell local products close to the maker. Tourism is for everyone, from tents to hotels. Farm shops and farm campsites strengthen the local economy.

261817.explanation: Sometimes it can be necessary to say 'no' under certain weather conditions. We are AGAINST a BAN on wood stoves and fireplaces. Residents retain freedom of choice. Filters and better burning are better than banning. Some residents depend on a wood stove. Gas is expensive; we want to combat energy poverty.

261818.explanation: Don't look only at benefits, but at what people actually have to spend, including other residents with low/minimum incomes. The Zutphen Pass helps with this. This motion from Kies Bewust Lokaal has already been adopted. Sport is good to combat loneliness and good for health.

261820.explanation: Bouncers (BOA's) may investigate, issue fines, and conduct checks. They enforce minor violations and thus relieve the police. With good briefings, sufficient visible BOAs, and clear, fair enforcement, they ensure safety on the street so that public spaces remain clean, safe, and pleasant.

261821.explanation: Kies Bewust Lokaal makes this a top priority. We ensure quick follow-up of reports, fixed contact persons per neighborhood, and good cooperation with housing corporations, police, and aid services. Everyone has the right to a quiet neighborhood and must feel seen and heard.

261822.explanation: Camera surveillance can be used in dangerous places to safeguard the safety of residents, police, and security personnel. It is worrying that camera surveillance is needed because certain people misbehave and unsafe situations arise. It should not be used to limit freedom!

261823.explanation: Kies Bewust Lokaal believes that every road user with undesirable behavior in traffic should be addressed. There should be more frequent checks.

261824.explanation: Therefore, Kies Bewust Lokaal chooses a fair and realistic housing policy. Homes are distributed fairly; our residents are given priority for housing within Zutphen, Warnsveld, and the Hoven. No priority for people living in our municipality inside prison walls, asylum seeker centers, or psychiatric institutions.

261825.explanation: What the BOAs have reported back is that they are satisfied with a body camera. They do not want a baton. They focus on supervision and enforcement. The police are there to (in exceptional cases) appropriately use a baton.

261826.explanation: Whether you walk, cycle, drive a car, or use public transport, that choice is personal. It is the municipality's task to organize these facilities properly, not to block or exclude people. Low-car means not car-free center (sufficient parking space is necessary).

261827.explanation: The housing deal describes the agreements made nationally and provincially. Kies Bewust Lokaal certainly wants more affordable housing for young people and seniors. 1,900 residents are waiting for a (different) social rental home. This must be a priority. Then the middle segment also gets space.

261828.explanation: Low-car means not car-free. In consultation with residents, it should be considered whether certain narrow streets can be made low-car. Homes must always be accessible by car (groceries, moving vans, mobility impairments, etc.). Delivery services speed through the city. This must change! Think of entrepreneurs!

261829.explanation: Entrepreneurs have the right, just like residents, to live free of debt. Especially after corona, many entrepreneurs have been the victims of The Hague’s policies. Entrepreneurs must be supported if they provide good reasons for wanting debt restructuring.

261830.explanation: This is very simplistic. Prices must be the same throughout the municipality. The Hoven and Warnsveld. All neighborhoods count just as much! Not only the inner city. Clear conditions so that a permit can be issued quickly with less bureaucracy.

261831.explanation: Adjusting speed should not be an end in itself. If safety improves, speed can be lowered. Always in consultation with current residents of the street and users of the street. The ring around the center must also remain accessible for all transport.

261832.explanation: Residents must be involved early and properly. Not only at the end for formality but from the start. In major changes, such as a merger, residents have the right to give their opinion and for it to weigh heavily in decision-making. A referendum is best then.

261833.explanation: It is always good to involve children young in politics. However, we must ensure that it truly contributes to society.

261834.explanation: To reduce the gap/distance between residents and the municipal government. Kies Bewust Lokaal thinks this can happen more often. Promised, but not fulfilled by the coalition in the past four years. We will implement this immediately! Truly involve residents. Politics close by!

261836.explanation: Kies Bewust Lokaal focuses on local entrepreneurship. This is good for our economy and employment. However, we need to consider how this should be supervised. There are also co-working buildings where both start-ups and established entrepreneurs are located.

261837.explanation: We want the local entrepreneur to be involved as much as possible. However, there is legislation and regulation that allow nationwide tenders. No political party can guarantee otherwise. Let's be honest and not sell fairy tales. A local company is preferred.

261838.explanation: Currently, you must apply for an event permit no later than 12 weeks before the event. This is due to laws, regulations, and safety measures. Kies Bewust Lokaal believes it is important that this process is careful and not longer than necessary. It should be efficient and safe.

261839.explanation: Waste is part of daily life. Therefore, waste policy must be logical, clear, and fair. If you want people to separate waste properly, you have to make it easy. If you want to prevent dumping, you have to help, not hinder.

261840.explanation: The hospitality sector ensures vibrancy and economy. Have one fixed time (7 days a week) when terraces are open so everyone knows what to expect. Provided that the hospitality sector actively prevents nuisance and respects residents.

261841.explanation: Our municipality has been one of the most heavily burdened in the region for years. Zutphen hosts an asylum seeker center for 740 residents, temporary shelter for Ukrainians, multiple mental health institutions, addiction care, a prison and probation service, protected housing, and social shelter. Enough is enough!

261842.explanation: We are already doing enough. Our municipality has been one of the most heavily burdened in the region for years. Zutphen hosts an asylum seeker center for 740 residents, temporary shelter for Ukrainians, multiple mental health institutions, addiction care, a prison and probation service, protected housing, and social shelter, etc.

261843.explanation: We focus on residents who have lived here longer and have long waiting times. It is not a fair situation if we give them priority. 1,900 residents of our municipality have been waiting for a home for years. With the influx of many refugees, they will never get a home. The national government must solve this.

261844.explanation: Hands off our traditions! This fireworks show will always remain as long as Kies Bewust Lokaal has a say. It is a connecting activity during the fair for many residents. Fireworks have also become much more environmentally friendly. The fireworks industry has invested heavily in this.

261846.explanation: The municipality provides storage capacity in neighborhoods. If residents want a home battery themselves, the municipality provides information and explanation. Subsidies come first from Europe, The Hague, or the province.

261847.explanation: They certainly must be open to combat loneliness and allow people to meet each other in the evening. Also start opportunities in every community center to organize youth meeting places. Therefore, community centers must also be open in the evening.

261848.explanation: Waste is part of daily life. Therefore, waste policy must be logical, clear, and fair. The gray container remains at the home, without extra costs. Residents have free choice.

261849.explanation: Everyone who undertakes with respect for nature must have the opportunity to do so.

261850.explanation: For a municipality like Zutphen, a good swimming pool should be realized for the residents of Zutphen, Warnsveld, and de Hoven. Including an outdoor pool and play area. However, cost-benefit analysis must be considered. Involve the residents.

261851.explanation: Hands off the traditions. The Easter fire is once a year. However, the weather and safety must be taken into account. It creates connection and meeting among residents and is a tradition we can be proud of.

261852.explanation: For Kies Bewust Lokaal, green neighborhoods, healthy air, and space for nature in new construction are self-evident. Nevertheless, we guard against even more regulations because that further delays housing construction. There are sufficient national regulations.

261853.explanation: Hanzehuis can become a strong cultural venue where theater, music education, and the library collaborate. Culture is important, investments are good but must be fair, transparent, and sensible. The council ('18-'22) had a plan for 15 million euros, but due to a new plan, it has more than doubled. Absurd. Kies Bewust Lokaal will not spend more than 35 million on it.

261854.explanation: Give young people a voice. Let the youth council organize a youth assembly. Also start opportunities in every community center to organize youth meeting places.

261855.explanation: Lower rent is negotiable if more of the accommodation can be shared with other activities to fill empty hours. Kies Bewust Lokaal has arranged by motion that there will be a Zutphen Pass. This must be implemented urgently. This allows residents, among other things, to participate in sports cheaply.

261856.explanation: Not only must we invest in a university of applied sciences (HBO), but also in vocational schools and students who drop out. Bringing education to Zutphen also ensures that young people settle more and will attract entrepreneurs who promote internships, work, socializing, and nightlife for young people.

261857.explanation: To promote equal opportunities, this is important. The municipal council has already reserved much money to counter language disadvantage and tackle low literacy. This must not be cut; it must be safeguarded in schools.

261858.explanation: Illegal waste dumping must be strictly tackled. Make waste policy logical, clear, and fair. Provide opportunities in every neighborhood to dispose of bulky waste. Not everyone has transport to bring waste themselves. Waste is part of daily life. If you don't want dumping, provide options!

261859.explanation: A completely new neighborhood will not be created. Kies Bewust Lokaal believes the landscape character must be preserved. There is also talk of turning recreation park Bronsbergen into a neighborhood. It is currently tolerated. We want clarity, yes, provided that all costs are borne by the owners.

261860.explanation: We have sufficient housing companies within the municipality. With agreements from the adopted housing vision, we can focus on sufficient affordable housing. Ensure the permit policy is also addressed so that it is easy to realize cheaper, sustainable homes for housing companies.

261861.explanation: Unnecessary vacancy must be tackled. To counter unnecessary vacancy, the permit policy must also be addressed. Too expensive, too lengthy. Vacancy where a building deteriorates must be prevented, even if with fines. Preventing vacancy must be encouraged.

261863.explanation: What Kies Bewust Lokaal wants to achieve is to get the housing flow going. Whether simply providing money is the solution is questionable. First, more affordable housing must be built for this target group, including ground-level homes. Moving must be voluntary. Maintaining the same housing costs is more important.
