261864.explanation: We focus on flexible informal care homes. If there is a desire, it must be possible but not mandatory. Homes we build must be future-proof and possibly divisible.

261865.explanation: Residents who want to build homes themselves together must be supported. This can be a group of starters or elderly people. They then act as their own project developers. This creates affordable housing and connected neighborhoods.

261866.explanation: This must be determined very carefully. The city center remains preserved as it is now. In some places, higher buildings are possible. This is real customization and must be properly addressed through citizen participation. Given the housing shortage, we will have to build upwards in certain locations.

261867.explanation: This is already regulated by law. From January 1, 2025, landlords are required to include a points evaluation with a new rental contract. Also, municipalities must now enforce against excessive rents.

261868.explanation: It currently works well. It must be checked if there is a solid reason underlying it. There is a limit. Ensure they do not fall back into debt; address the entire system for the resident involved, otherwise they will relapse and start over. That must be prevented.

261869.explanation: This must be organized for all residents of Zutphen. Also among young people, families, and the elderly. Especially to prevent debts and debt restructuring. Quality over quantity.

261871.explanation: Kies Bewust Lokaal believes that everyone in Zutphen should be able to live, not just survive. Therefore, we choose clear and fair solutions. A proper start. A proper start ensures equal opportunities. Good food, vegetables, meat, and drinks for every child.

261872.explanation: If you want to make an area car-reduced, you must first invest in parking garages. Thanks to one of our motions, we are now working on this. Also for cyclists to safely park their bikes. Underground parking equals building above ground and creating space with greenery. Relieves parking pressure, but must be affordable.

261873.explanation: Only if you first invest in parking (parking garages). Thanks to one of our motions, we are now working on this. Also for cyclists to safely park their bikes. Underground parking means building above ground and creating space with greenery. You relieve parking pressure; this must be affordable.

261874.explanation: Through the municipality, residents with a low income can take out health insurance with dental coverage. This way you choose your own dentist. Master of your own mouth.

261876.explanation: Youth workers are not there to solve real mental health problems. Specialized care is needed for that. However, Kies Bewust Lokaal wants to strengthen youth work in every neighborhood. Young people need safe meeting places where they can be themselves.

261816.explanation: We want to give farmers space to make their businesses future-proof, for example with a shop or small-scale recreation. But this must fit the landscape, environment, and livability. No automatic permission, but customized solutions.

261817.explanation: Under unfavorable weather conditions, wood burning can lead to severe air pollution and health complaints. The municipality may intervene to protect vulnerable residents, with clear rules and good information.

261818.explanation: Sports and exercise are important for health and social participation. For people with welfare benefits, money should not be a barrier. Therefore, the municipality must support sports participation through existing schemes and customized solutions.

261820.explanation: Safety requires visible and approachable enforcement officers in neighborhoods, the city center, and villages. We observe that the police are increasingly withdrawing from maintaining public order. We believe that expanding the number of municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) is necessary and that increased deployment can contribute to livability.

261821.explanation: However, the municipality cannot enforce this, but can actively help organize and stimulate it. The neighborhood police officer must be visible and reachable for his or her area. We also see a greater role here for the (neighborhood) BOA.

261822.explanation: Permanent camera surveillance is not compatible with an open and livable municipality. Temporary camera surveillance may only be deployed in case of demonstrable nuisance, proportionally and with respect for privacy.

261823.explanation: The police should focus on serious safety issues and crime. Antisocial behavior in traffic primarily calls for education, awareness, and targeted deployment of enforcement officers, not extra police supervision.

261824.explanation: We do want a maximum of 50% of the homes to be reserved for residents of Zutphen to ensure that young people and those looking to move within the city can continue to live here.

261825.explanation: The baton may only be used for self-defense. BOAs do not use force to enforce rules; that remains a police responsibility. Deployment must be strictly regulated and well-trained.

261826.explanation: We stand for a low-car but easily accessible city center. Parking on the outskirts and smart use of existing facilities ensure that cars are not prohibited while the city center remains livable.

261827.explanation: Affordable housing is essential, but a fixed percentage of social rental housing is not always the best solution. We choose at least 70% affordable homes, with a mix of social rental, mid-range rental, and affordable owner-occupied housing, fitting the neighborhood and demand.

261828.explanation: More car-free streets can make the city center more livable and safer. This especially applies to through-traffic. Residents, deliveries, and emergency services must still have access.

261829.explanation: Debt assistance must also be accessible to entrepreneurs. Where possible, help should focus on recovery and continuity so that viable businesses can continue to exist.

261830.explanation: Permits should be affordable but not equal for everyone. Social and non-commercial initiatives deserve a different rate than commercial events. This way, we preserve space for culture and social interaction without unfair subsidies to commercial parties.

261831.explanation: Lower speed limits increase safety in residential neighborhoods, villages, and the city center. We must maintain good traffic flow on main roads.

261832.explanation: A merger is a drastic decision that requires early and serious involvement of residents. A referendum is not the only or best tool. We choose broad participation, transparent decision-making, and a clear role for the municipal council.

261833.explanation: A children's mayor helps involve children early in our municipality. It is an accessible way to encourage participation and engagement.

261834.explanation: The municipal council must be visible and approachable in neighborhoods and villages. This is better achieved through neighborhood visits, conversations, and office hours than formal council meetings outside the town hall.

261836.explanation: Starting entrepreneurs must be given space to grow their business. A temporary exemption from property tax (ozb) helps with this and strengthens the local economy.

261837.explanation: By letting local companies carry out construction work as much as possible, jobs and expertise remain in Zutphen. This strengthens the local economy and fosters involvement in projects.

261838.explanation: Permit applications must be handled fairly and transparently. Parties and festivals are important but should not automatically get priority over other applications. Equal rules for everyone ensure clarity and trust.

261839.explanation: Those who produce more residual waste should pay more, which is better for the environment and costs. The incentive to separate waste and reduce residual waste must remain.

261840.explanation: Terraces are important for the city's liveliness, but extended opening hours can cause nuisance for residents. The municipality must carefully weigh each location, considering livability and tranquility.

261841.explanation: Zutphen already takes responsibility in asylum seeker reception. Further reception requires a fair national distribution and spreading across municipalities. New reception in Zutphen can only happen in consultation with residents and with sufficient support.

261842.explanation: Only if necessary. It concerns temporary reception, not structural expansion. This can only be done in consultation with residents and with clear agreements about duration and location.

261843.explanation: Everyone must be treated equally in the allocation of social rental housing. Priority based on status does not fit here. Good guidance and accommodation for refugees must be part of regular housing policy.

261844.explanation: The municipality must work towards a sustainable alternative to fireworks. As long as there is no affordable and nature-friendly alternative with comparable impact, fireworks may continue at the fair. Active search for replacement is required.

261846.explanation: Neighborhood batteries help better utilize locally generated energy. The municipality can support and co-finance this, together with resident initiatives and energy cooperatives.

261847.explanation: Community centers are important for meeting and cohesion, also during evening hours. By contributing to extended opening hours, the municipality helps prevent loneliness and strengthens the neighborhood.

261848.explanation: The gray bin for residual waste must remain. At the same time, we continue to look for other ways to reduce and better separate waste.

261849.explanation: Organic farming contributes to soil quality, biodiversity, and a healthy living environment. This should therefore be weighed more heavily when leasing municipal land.

261850.explanation: Swimming pool De IJsselslag is important for sports, relaxation, and social contact. Expansion with a play area and outdoor pool can increase attractiveness, provided it is affordable and meets residents' needs.

261851.explanation: Easter fires pose risks to nature, air quality, and safety. The municipality must therefore prohibit these and focus on safe and appropriate alternatives for social gatherings and tradition.

261852.explanation: Not more rules, but better application of existing rules provides sufficient space for nature in new construction. Quality and enforcement are more important than constantly introducing new regulations.

261853.explanation: The Hanzehuis must not become a prestige project, but bundling cultural functions is necessary. By also housing the library lending function here from the Broederenkerk, functions can be combined and costs saved.

261854.explanation: Young people in Zutphen also need their own safe meeting place. A youth center can contribute to welfare, social contact, and preventing nuisance, provided it is developed together with the youth.

261855.explanation: Sports clubs are independent and vary greatly in costs and possibilities. Setting membership fees is their own responsibility. The municipality can support sports through tailor-made solutions, such as targeted rent reductions or support where needed.

261856.explanation: There is already sufficient higher professional education (HBO) in the region. Instead of an expensive campaign, we prefer cooperation with existing HBO institutions in the region and provisions that bind young people to Zutphen.

261857.explanation: Language development is important, but good facilities already exist for early childhood education. Instead of new separate schemes, the municipality must ensure that existing support works well and reaches everyone who needs it.

261858.explanation: Waste beside containers causes nuisance and annoyance among residents. Enforcement must be given higher priority to keep public spaces clean and livable.

261859.explanation: Permanent living is currently tolerated in Bronsbergen, but converting it into a full residential area would cost a lot and is too far from Zutphen's existing buildings. This makes it an unsuitable location for a new residential area.

261860.explanation: Building and renting out homes is not a core task of the municipality. This role belongs to housing corporations. The municipality must focus on conditions, cooperation, and governance, not become a landlord itself.

261861.explanation: Long-term vacancy is unacceptable in times of housing shortage. The municipality must address this through the housing regulations and a vacancy ordinance, so homes become available again.

261863.explanation: Flow through housing is not achieved with money, but by offering affordable and suitable homes. The municipality must provide appropriate living arrangements for the elderly, making moving attractive and logical.

261864.explanation: Living with or near family can be valuable, but it must fit the needs and the neighborhood. The municipality should enable this in new construction, not make it mandatory for every project.

261865.explanation: Building together gives residents more control over their homes and neighborhood. Therefore, the municipality should designate places for collective housing initiatives, such as CPO projects, as stated in our election program.

261866.explanation: A fixed maximum height limit does not fit everywhere. The environment is decisive. New construction must always consider the view and character of the historic city center, as can be recorded in a height vision.

261867.explanation: Support for tenants is important, but establishing a municipal rental team is not a direct task of the municipality. The municipality must ensure good referrals to existing agencies and supervise enforcement where necessary.

261868.explanation: Quick and clear debt assistance prevents problems from worsening. The municipality can take a coordinating role by bundling debts as much as possible, so residents gain overview and can work on one arrangement.

261869.explanation: Learning to manage money is important, but this guidance primarily belongs to organizations like COA and Refugee Work. The municipality must ensure good cooperation and referrals, not take over this task itself.

261871.explanation: Good nutrition for young children is important, but isolated schemes like a free vegetable bag are not a structural solution. The municipality would do better to invest in existing youth health care facilities and support for parents.

261872.explanation: Underground parking garages are expensive and risky, especially in a historic city like Zutphen. We choose for an accessible city with parking at the edges and better use of existing (parking) facilities, as stated in our election program.

261873.explanation: ’s Gravenhof can grow into a pleasant car-free square with appeal for Zutphen. Fewer cars provide more space for staying, events, and the quality of the historic city center.

261874.explanation: Dental care for children up to 18 is covered by the basic health insurance package. Therefore, a dental bus is not a task of the municipality. However, the municipality and schools can focus on good education about oral care.

261876.explanation: Mental problems among youth require attention nearby. Youth workers can detect, support, and refer. This is an important part of our election program and preventive youth policy.

261816.explanation: The CDA is in favor of more space for other business activities around farms.

261817.explanation: A ban on the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves creates a lot of regulatory pressure and is difficult to enforce. However, we want good information about the consequences of wood burning during unsuitable weather.

261818.explanation: This already happens, namely in the form of the child package and the Participation scheme. The CDA has supported the increase of these schemes.

261820.explanation: We want more visible safety on the streets. Enforcement officers are necessary for this, especially due to shortages in the police force.

261821.explanation: We want police officers and neighborhood enforcement officers to be more visible in the neighborhood. Weekly office hours are very important here.

261822.explanation: The CDA wants more camera surveillance in areas with nuisance. Besides the Schupstoel, this also includes the station area.

261823.explanation: If there is nuisance from people on fatbikes, we believe this should be enforced against.

261824.explanation: We want priority for people with local ties so that people can continue to live in Zutphen, Warnsveld, and De Hoven.

261825.explanation: In our opinion, this is not necessary at this moment.

261826.explanation: Good accessibility of the city center by car is vital for our local economy.

261827.explanation: In most places in Zutphen and Warnsveld, 25 to 40% of the new homes must be social rental housing. We find this sufficient. Additionally, we want to be able to build enough affordable owner-occupied homes.

261828.explanation: The CDA currently does not want more car-free streets in the city center because the car is an important means of transport for many city center residents and visitors.

261829.explanation: We support good help for entrepreneurs who get into trouble. This statement is too unclear for us to make a proper assessment here.

261830.explanation: The CDA wants to stimulate the arrival of more parties and events in the city center. This can be done, among others, by lowering permit fees.

261831.explanation: The CDA wants to lower the maximum speed only if the road surface matches the speed limit.

261832.explanation: The future of our municipality is too important to reduce to a yes or no question. We prefer to involve residents in ways that allow them to give more detailed opinions.

261833.explanation: A children's mayor involves peers (groups 7 and 8) in politics and indirectly teaches them about how our democracy works.

261834.explanation: The CDA wants the municipal council to be more visible in the neighborhoods, for instance by sometimes holding meetings in the neighborhoods.

261836.explanation: If this is legally possible, we consider this a good way to stimulate employment and entrepreneurship.

261837.explanation: Outsourcing large construction projects to local entrepreneurs is not always legally possible.

261838.explanation: We want more events in Zutphen. We support effective measures that contribute to this.

261839.explanation: We find it fairer if people pay for the amount of waste they throw away; therefore, we want to maintain the current system of differentiated rates.

261840.explanation: If this can be done responsibly, the CDA wants terraces to remain open longer. This keeps a vibrant city center.

261841.explanation: Zutphen currently accommodates three times as many asylum seekers as legally required. Therefore, additional permanent shelter is not currently on the agenda. If our current shelter becomes insufficient in the future, we will of course take responsibility.

261842.explanation: Fortunately, Zutphen already takes its responsibility in asylum reception. A second asylum center where people from Ter Apel temporarily stay is therefore not currently on the agenda. If the situation in Ter Apel or globally changes, we will of course take responsibility to accommodate more.

261843.explanation: The municipality is obliged to provide housing for refugees with a residence permit (status holders). However, we believe status holders should not have priority over other vulnerable groups, such as the homeless or divorced parents.

261844.explanation: The fireworks show during the fair is a beautiful tradition that attracts many visitors. The CDA also wants a municipal fireworks display during New Year's Eve.

261846.explanation: We are open to subsidies that enable sustainability. This could include co-financing neighborhood batteries.

261847.explanation: We want to make it easier for people to meet each other; longer opening hours of community centers can help with this.

261848.explanation: This way we prevent more litter. The gray bin is also better for people who have difficulty walking 250 meters to a central point.

261849.explanation: We support organic farming, but for now we do not want to impose extra municipal regulations on farmers. At the moment, it is important that national and European rules for farmers are properly enforced.

261850.explanation: We want the swimming pool to become more enjoyable for people to swim. We do this by providing more opportunities to play. This way the swimming pool might also become more profitable.

261851.explanation: Easter fires are rare in our municipality, so a ban is unnecessary. In addition, the CDA wants to reduce the regulatory burden in society.

261852.explanation: There are already many good rules that ensure green new-build neighborhoods, and the CDA finds those rules sufficient.

261853.explanation: We believe the current Hanzehof needs renovation. However, we find the current renovation plans (over 30 million) too costly. Therefore, we want to get the most out of it by, among other things, fully incorporating the library into the Hanzehuis.

261854.explanation: The CDA wants more places where young people can meet each other. However, we believe young people themselves should decide what such a youth center looks like; the municipality can help with funding and space.

261855.explanation: We support associations but do not see this measure as adding value.

261856.explanation: The CDA has successfully advocated for this. Additionally, we want more vocational education (MBO) programs to be offered in our municipality.

261857.explanation: Extra language lessons are very important to prevent children from later developing learning delays at primary school; we do this through early childhood education. The CDA believes early childhood education should be free.

261858.explanation: Dumping waste next to underground containers causes a lot of nuisance. The CDA wants to enforce stricter measures against this.

261859.explanation: The hamlet Bronsbergen is a place where nature and agriculture come together, and we want to preserve that. However, we want to build more upwards, in De Hoven and Noordveen.

261860.explanation: We believe housing associations are very good at building and renting affordable rental homes. This is not a task for the municipality.

261861.explanation: We support introducing a vacancy tax to encourage property owners to give their properties a function.

261863.explanation: Family homes are often sold with large capital gains, so the CDA does not see a bonus as necessary. We do want seniors who rent socially to be able to move to a smaller home, keeping their old, lower rent.

261864.explanation: Caring for each other should become easier. For those who want it, it should be possible to let parents or grandparents live in, while maintaining privacy.

261865.explanation: The CDA wants to give space to residents who have plans to live together in alternative ways, such as courtyards for seniors, tiny houses, or mobile homes.

261866.explanation: We do not want to affect the skyline of the medieval city center. However, we do want to build high in other places, as long as livability is not put under pressure.

261867.explanation: A rental team, like those in Zwolle and The Hague, can ensure tenants more easily receive support.

261868.explanation: Having one creditor provides more peace and clarity.

261869.explanation: The CDA wants to invest in good and compassionate integration of refugees allowed to stay in the Netherlands. This includes managing finances wisely and assisting with finding suitable employment.

261871.explanation: The CDA does not consider this a task for the municipality.

261872.explanation: If above-ground parking spaces disappear, then an underground parking garage is vital for our local economy.

261873.explanation: The CDA does not want to remove parking spaces in the city center as long as there is no good alternative.

261874.explanation: Dental care is already free for children, though many parents do not know this. The CDA wants to provide education so more parents know their child can go to the dentist for free.

261876.explanation: The CDA finds youth workers very important. Besides help with mental health issues, youth workers also help prevent crime and drug addiction.

261816.explanation: The Christian Union prefers to give farmers space. However, what is sold in the store must be related to the farm. The store or campsite should also fit within nature, landscape, and surroundings.

261817.explanation: Clean air is vital for everyone, says the Christian Union. Zutphen is affiliated with the Clean Air Agreement. Residents can download the Stookwijzer app to check if it is a suitable time to light the stove or fireplace.

261818.explanation: Sports should be accessible to everyone. Money should not be a barrier to participate. Residents on welfare can pay for sports activities via Zutphen’s Participation Scheme. For more expensive sports, it should be considered whether funding can come from other sources.

261820.explanation: We must tackle nuisance in Zutphen. We should also look at the reasons why situations escalate. Therefore, the Christian Union wants to keep investing in the social cohesion of neighborhoods and act preventively.

261821.explanation: It is important that neighborhood officers are visible and accessible. Having consultation hours ensures the officer is known in their neighborhood. This fits within the police policy.

261822.explanation: Many people feel unsafe around the Schupstoel in the evening. A camera may help in that case. The Christian Union considers such a camera a temporary solution.

261823.explanation: The Christian Union finds traffic safety very important. If road users do not follow the rules, they should receive a fine.

261824.explanation: Zutphen’s Housing Ordinance must be adjusted for this, says the Christian Union. But we must comply with national legislation, for example regarding the mandatory housing of status holders.

261825.explanation: By-law enforcement officers have the task to address and enforce where things go wrong. They can use their panic button if necessary. According to the Christian Union, further arming would provoke more escalation.

261826.explanation: Accessibility remains always important, says the Christian Union. The city center must offer more space to pedestrians and cyclists and remain accessible to the disabled. With this in mind, we want to advocate for building a parking garage in the center.

261827.explanation: Thirty percent is quite normal; Zutphen already sits at 31%. The Christian Union wants to combat mismatched housing and focus more on affordable owner-occupied homes. This promotes housing turnover.

261828.explanation: More car-free streets, like 's Gravenhof, Lange Hofstraat, and the markets, improve the livability, safety, and quality of stay in the city center. The Christian Union chooses a green city!

261829.explanation: The municipality does not need to bear the entrepreneur’s risk. The municipality can offer help and perspective to get out of debt. The Christian Union will continue to work to lower the threshold for asking for help.

261830.explanation: Permit costs can possibly be reduced. Costs for, for example, safety and other obligations are increasingly expensive for organizers. The Christian Union believes the municipality should not make a profit on permits.

261831.explanation: This increases traffic safety and livability in residential neighborhoods. The ChristianUnion is a strong supporter of this.

261832.explanation: Residents should be involved in time, but a referendum is not the best tool for this. The ChristianUnion prefers to engage in conversation with residents about such major matters. That way, we reach a good balance.

261833.explanation: Involving children in city governance strengthens citizenship and engagement. The ChristianUnion believes that teaching young leads to lifelong habits.

261834.explanation: The ChristianUnion wants to bring politics closer to the residents. This is an ideal tool for that. We look forward to meeting residents in this way.

261836.explanation: Starting entrepreneurs deserve support from the municipality. That is why the ChristianUnion wants them to be exempt from property tax (ozb) during their first year. After that first year, they will pay.

261837.explanation: The ChristianUnion holds the local economy dear. Within the rules of fair procurement, we certainly see opportunities to promote the local economy.

261838.explanation: Permits must be handled fairly and carefully.

261839.explanation: The ChristianUnion adheres to the principle that the polluter pays. Responsible waste management should be rewarded.

261840.explanation: Owners of cafes and restaurants already receive a lot of space from the municipality to keep their terraces open. The ChristianUnion also values the livability of residents in the city center.

261841.explanation: The ChristianUnion can and wants to take responsibility within reasonable and humane limits. It is very important that residents are involved in the preparation and decision-making regarding new housing.

261842.explanation: The ChristianUnion is open to temporary additional shelter if necessary. For example, shelter in the Hanzehal. In the past, the reception of Syrian refugees in 2015 went very well. Zutphen can handle this.

261843.explanation: There is a nationally imposed scheme with a quota that municipalities must meet. The ChristianUnion is proud of Zutphen because we have met that quota annually for years.

261844.explanation: Zutphen has banned the use of fireworks within its municipal boundaries in 2024. Everyone must adhere to this, including at the fair. The ChristianUnion supports this ban.

261846.explanation: The ChristianUnion is very pleased with all the energy generated by private individuals. Unfortunately, the grid cannot handle it. Local energy storage would increase support for acquiring solar panels. We also hope this form of storage raises awareness.

261847.explanation: The ChristianUnion greatly appreciates all the work done in and around community centers. Community centers themselves are responsible for evening opening hours. The municipality cannot demand this.

261848.explanation: The pilot in the South Districts showed that people had less residual waste when using the underground container. Proper waste separation then becomes more necessary. The ChristianUnion sees another advantage: neighbors can help each other.

261849.explanation: The municipality must actively stimulate sustainable land use. Zutphen wants to become the bio-city of the Netherlands. Positive support is therefore essential! The ChristianUnion sees a future for organic community farms.

261850.explanation: The IJsselslag remains a financial concern. Do people prefer open water (Bronsbergen and Bussloo) or outdoor pools in Almen, Gorssel, Steenderen, and Vorden? There are plenty of facilities in the area.

261851.explanation: Easter fires are forbidden in Zutphen. Safety and nature take precedence. That perfectly aligns with the ChristianUnion.

261852.explanation: Greenery and biodiversity must be a structural part of construction. The rules should not be patronizing. Given climate change, we face challenges with heat, rain, and prolonged drought. A green neighborhood contributes to a better living environment.

261853.explanation: Culture and social interaction deserve a place in the city. The Hanzehuis should bring all generations together. Experiencing culture together contributes to connection, says the ChristianUnion.

261854.explanation: Young people need safe meeting places. The ChristianUnion believes a youth center works best as a youth initiative: by young people, for young people, with municipal support.

261855.explanation: The ChristianUnion finds it very important that sports clubs remain affordable. Residents can apply for the municipality's Participation scheme. The municipality itself has no influence on rent levels because it has been outsourced to Zutphen Aktief.

261856.explanation: This has been tried repeatedly without success. The ChristianUnion does not find it realistic to invest money here again. Saxion, HAN, and Windesheim are a short train ride from Zutphen. We prefer to invest in companies that offer, for example, master-apprentice training.

261857.explanation: Early language development prevents problems later in life. There is ample time available for Early Childhood Education in Zutphen. The ChristianUnion wants to focus more on reaching parents. Prevention is the biggest improvement here.

261858.explanation: Enforcement is necessary. Everyone must also clearly know where to dispose of waste. A suggestion from the ChristianUnion: give residents the space to maintain a beautiful garden around the containers. This works very well in Rotterdam.

261859.explanation: The residential area is already included in Zutphen’s environmental vision as a densification location. This means that homes are already planned.

261860.explanation: This causes a lot of work and pressures the municipal budget. It is questionable whether rents will be lower as a result. Moreover, the ChristianUnion believes the municipality is not and should not become a business.

261861.explanation: Vacancies are unacceptable with a housing shortage. The municipality must strictly monitor this.

261863.explanation: This measure could stimulate mobility in the housing market. The ChristianUnion is very pleased with the relocation coach in Zutphen. This coach can also encourage and support elderly people to move to a smaller home.

261864.explanation: Living together strengthens care and social cohesion. This is a priority for the ChristianUnion. We believe that new construction projects should especially focus on alternative living arrangements.

261865.explanation: This stimulates community building and customization. The municipality should not only designate locations where residents can build together but especially reduce the number of rules for this. The complicated regulations in Zutphen are a thorn in the side of the ChristianUnion.

261866.explanation: High-rise buildings require careful consideration per location, so customization. For example, the Sterflat in Zutphen is 13 floors high. That can be there. The ChristianUnion finds lower high-rise buildings more appropriate in the city center.

261867.explanation: Tenants must be protected against abuses. In Warnsveld, the tenants' consultation Warnsveld-Zutphen is active. This tenant team could be expanded to Zutphen to assist all municipality tenants. The municipality must facilitate this.

261868.explanation: The resident is responsible for their own household budget. In some cases, the municipality wants to take over the rehabilitated debt (after coordination with all creditors). The resident must repay this debt to the municipality anyway and is required to work with a Budget Buddy.

261869.explanation: The ChristianUnion is happy that this already happens. Status holders receive an introduction program explaining all ins and outs of Dutch financial obligations. Good financial guidance prevents problems.

261871.explanation: Children are introduced to healthy food at a young age. Within the framework of Chance-rich Start: the first 1,000 days, and JOGG (Youth at a Healthy Weight), this bag contributes to equal opportunities for all. Parents are thus given the chance to familiarize children with fresh vegetables.

261872.explanation: An underground parking garage has been a long-standing wish of the ChristianUnion. If you want to keep the inner city lively and attractive for residents and tourists, a car-reduced city center with an underground parking garage is necessary. No cars in sight!

261873.explanation: 's Gravenhof should become a square with lots of greenery and cooling, where we can meet each other. Let's first make Zutphen's most beautiful square car-free; it is currently full of cars.

261874.explanation: Good oral care is important. All children have the right to free dental care. Many parents avoid it out of fear of potential costs. With a dental bus, all children get their turn. Prevention results in lower costs later in life.

261876.explanation: Prevention and proximity are very important for the well-being of young people. We are concerned about the high number of suicides among youth, especially young women. The municipality encourages working with experts by experience. The ChristenUnie finds this approach particularly important.

261816.explanation: We want space for farmers, entrepreneurship, and local products.

261817.explanation: We do not want a burning ban in the municipality. We leave this decision to the residents themselves.

261818.explanation: We see no reasons for the municipality to take this action.

261820.explanation: We want to lobby for more fully qualified police officers. BOAs are no substitute for that.

261821.explanation: Direct contact with the police can greatly contribute to the residents’ sense of safety.

261822.explanation: We believe physical presence of officers or enforcement is preferable to camera surveillance.

261823.explanation: Fatbikes are responsible for much nuisance, so they should be more strictly controlled as well.

261824.explanation: We ensure that residents of our municipality are given priority for social rental housing and affordable homeownership. Where possible, residents also receive priority in their own town or neighborhood.

261825.explanation: We keep the monopoly on violence with the police.

261826.explanation: We do not agree with low-traffic policies.

261827.explanation: We strive for a rental market where supply and demand better match, with sufficient space for middle-income rent and private rentals. Social housing should remain a safety net for those in need, not a permanent dependency.

261829.explanation: This is a common barrier for entrepreneurs to enter debt counseling. We want to remove this barrier.

261830.explanation: We want to keep permits affordable for everyone.

261831.explanation: We are against unnecessary speed limits. More room for cars.

261832.explanation: We oppose municipal mergers, but if such a plan comes up, residents must be able to make a binding decision.

261833.explanation: We see no added value in a child mayor.

261834.explanation: Holding meetings on location is good for contact with residents.

261836.explanation: We want to stimulate entrepreneurship and business activity, and this is a chosen way to do so.

261837.explanation: We want to use local businesses wherever possible.

261839.explanation: We want an affordable waste fee for all residents.

261840.explanation: We want to give entrepreneurs ample freedom to decide on such operating hours themselves.

261841.explanation: We do not want to accommodate asylum seekers in the municipality.

261842.explanation: We do not want asylum reception in our municipality.

261843.explanation: We want to immediately abolish priority for status holders.

261844.explanation: This is a fun activity for the entire city.

261846.explanation: This is not a task for the municipality.

261847.explanation: Community centers are a binding factor for the neighborhood, and longer opening hours can help with this.

261849.explanation: All farmers must be treated equally.

261851.explanation: Easter fires are an important tradition that we must cherish and absolutely not forbid.

261852.explanation: We do not want to delay construction with even more unnecessary rules.

261854.explanation: We see no immediate necessity for this.

261855.explanation: This makes sports more accessible for everyone in the municipality.

261856.explanation: We do not think this is needed for the city.

261859.explanation: We do not see this as a chosen location for housing development.

261860.explanation: We can leave housing construction to the market.

261863.explanation: We want to facilitate seniors who want to downsize, but direct gifts seem the wrong choice to us.

261864.explanation: It should not be an obligation, but we see great potential in such forms of living.

261866.explanation: We do not want any further large-scale high-rise construction in our municipality.

261867.explanation: This is not a task for the municipality.

261868.explanation: This is not a task of the municipality.

261869.explanation: This is a personal responsibility for these people.

261871.explanation: We do not see this as a task for the municipality.

261872.explanation: We want to expand parking facilities in the city.

261874.explanation: This is not a task of the municipality.

261876.explanation: The mental health of young people is very important, and we must address this properly.

261816.explanation: Farmers deserve space to make their businesses future-proof, but not automatically. It must fit with the surroundings, traffic, and livability. Tailored solutions per location.

261817.explanation: Health comes first. The municipality must be able to intervene when air quality is poor.

261818.explanation: Hoi Zutphen goes a step further: free sports for young people (16-24 years) and 65-plus. Sport is prevention against stress, loneliness, and high healthcare costs.

261820.explanation: Safety requires visible and approachable presence of enforcement officers in all neighborhoods.

261821.explanation: The neighborhood police officer must be close by and accessible to residents.

261822.explanation: In case of structural nuisance, camera surveillance is needed, targeted, temporary, and with clear privacy rules.

261823.explanation: Dangerous and antisocial behavior must always be addressed, regardless of the mode of transport.

261824.explanation: We go further: people with essential professions, starters, and entrepreneurs get priority for all housing, rental and purchase. Hoi Zutphen chooses a clear and fair starting position: living and working belong together. Those who keep Zutphen running must also be able to live here.

261825.explanation: Enforcement officers increasingly face aggression. Those who enforce on behalf of the municipality must be able to protect themselves.

261826.explanation: The city center must remain accessible to residents, healthcare, entrepreneurs, and visitors.

261827.explanation: We do not choose a fixed percentage but for sufficient affordable housing per project, fitting the neighborhood.

261828.explanation: Car-free is not a goal in itself. The city center must remain lively and accessible.

261829.explanation: Helping entrepreneurs prevents bankruptcies and job losses.

261830.explanation: Events provide opportunities for meeting and liveliness. Costs must not block that.

261831.explanation: 30 km/h speed limit in residential streets and near schools, not automatically everywhere.

261832.explanation: You don’t make such a decision without residents. First support, then decisions.

261833.explanation: A children’s mayor can contribute to the involvement of children with their municipality and politics.

261834.explanation: Politics should be visible and close to residents.

261836.explanation: Starters must be able to grow without additional burdens.

261837.explanation: Municipal money is residents’ money and must work locally.

261838.explanation: Events are important, but care and safety remain leading.

261839.explanation: Those who produce more waste should pay more.

261840.explanation: Of course, the hospitality sector must be given space, but we must also consider local residents.

261841.explanation: Zutphen already makes a large contribution and more than meets its obligations. Shelter must be fairly distributed across the Netherlands.

261842.explanation: Zutphen already contributes significantly. Extra shelter increases the pressure on housing and other facilities. Other municipalities should take the lead first.

261843.explanation: Due to the severe housing shortage, we give priority to Zutphen residents. This creates calm and fairness in the housing market.

261844.explanation: Traditions can continue, but safety and costs come first. If the fireworks show is not safe or too expensive, the municipality must stop it.

261846.explanation: Smart storage makes the energy transition feasible.

261847.explanation: Meeting others prevents loneliness. That can happen in community centers, but also in association buildings and sports canteens. These already exist and can play a bigger role as meeting places.

261850.explanation: The swimming pool must have sufficient support for school swimming, swimming lessons, and club sports. If that is not currently possible, expansion or another solution is needed. Swim safety and accessibility are paramount.

261851.explanation: Traditions can continue, provided they are safe. The municipality must be able to intervene when air quality is poor.

261852.explanation: Green is important, but rules must not block building.

261853.explanation: The Hanzehuis is a large and expensive project that adds little to daily life in the neighborhoods. That money can be better used for small-scale facilities close to residents, such as meeting places, culture, and activities within the neighborhood itself.

261854.explanation: Young people need a safe place to meet each other, organize activities, and develop themselves. This does not have to be a large building; it can also be small-scale in neighborhoods, for example in existing buildings.

261856.explanation: Zutphen is too small for a full and broad higher professional education (hbo) offering. Young people benefit more from well-accessible education in Arnhem and Deventer. Therefore, we prefer to invest in better accessibility rather than in a campaign without a realistic chance of success.

261857.explanation: A good start prevents later problems.

261858.explanation: Tackling nuisance is part of livability.

261859.explanation: Housing construction is possible, but only if nature is preserved, access is well arranged, and facilities grow accordingly. Not an isolated neighborhood, but one fitting Zutphen.

261860.explanation: A new housing company does not solve the housing shortage. Rather focus on faster building by existing parties and less bureaucracy.

261861.explanation: Vacancy is unacceptable in these times of housing shortage.

261864.explanation: In major new housing projects, life-course suitable homes and family homes with space for informal care must be a fixed part of the overall plan, without mandatory numbers per block.

261865.explanation: And also give residents more say and room for their ideas.

261866.explanation: This is too general. The height must fit the surroundings.

261867.explanation: The rental team offers protection against abuses.

261868.explanation: This measure is too general. It can help, but only if there is sufficient customization.

261869.explanation: It prevents debts and promotes independence.

261871.explanation: Provided it is part of a broader approach to healthy nutrition for young children.

261872.explanation: An underground parking garage is not an end in itself but can be a solution if it demonstrably contributes to accessibility and livability.

261873.explanation: Making it completely car-free harms accessibility for residents, entrepreneurs, and healthcare.

261876.explanation: Early and low-threshold help prevents mild mental problems from developing into serious care and dropout.

261816.explanation: We believe in a locally driven economy and short food chains, where farm shops make local food easily accessible. Small-scale camping provides farmers with additional income and strengthens the quality of the countryside.

261817.explanation: Wood stoves can cause respiratory problems, especially when used improperly and at unfavorable burning times. Therefore, good information and clear rules are necessary.

261818.explanation: Sports and exercise are essential for both physical and mental health. Participation in a sports club must therefore be accessible to every resident and be included in a 'Zutphen pass'.

261820.explanation: Zutphen has relatively many enforcement officers (boa’s) compared to other municipalities, and this has not led to better results. We want the existing enforcement officers to have a stronger social and preventive role, focused on contact with residents. Order and safety start with a strong community.

261821.explanation: We want stronger community spirit through well-funded neighborhood centers as the first point of contact. The police are a last resort, and the neighborhood officer is already available in an accessible way.

261822.explanation: This is unnecessary and infringes on privacy. We believe in strengthening social control first before resorting to such measures.

261823.explanation: At the same time, fatbikes must be banned from the city center via the General Local Regulation (APV). LEF Zutphen also wants more checks to see if fatbikes and e-bikes are modified.

261824.explanation: As before, with social and economic ties. This way you receive extra allocation points when obtaining a home.

261825.explanation: There is no need to reinforce the spiral of violence. Therefore, enforcement officers must develop more social skills, such as de-escalation, seeking connection, and mediation. We want enforcers to be stronger in their role as caregivers. This promotes harmony and makes their work more effective.

261826.explanation: We believe the car should be subordinate to bicycle traffic, especially in the city center where more people arrive by bike than by car. At the same time, there must remain limited space for cars, for less mobile people who have no alternative and for entrepreneurs.

261827.explanation: More social rental housing is necessary. We also support the current policy of mixed construction, combining owner-occupied and social rental housing. This strengthens social cohesion and the livability of the municipality.

261828.explanation: We support the expansion of car-free zones, with exceptions for entrepreneurs and people with limited mobility. Everything in close consultation with the residents.

261829.explanation: Support for entrepreneurs is very important, both for start-up entrepreneurs and for those gradually winding down their business.

261830.explanation: Organizing a party or festival in Zutphen is unnecessarily expensive. The municipality must try to facilitate this as cheaply as possible.

261831.explanation: Slowing down is something LEF strongly supports. Zutphen is well suited for driving calmly; the municipality is small enough to reach everywhere at 30 km/h. Then biking is often faster and healthier.

261832.explanation: LEF is against merging with other municipalities; scaling up will create much distance between politics and residents. Cooperation with other municipalities benefits everyone more.

261833.explanation: More input and presence from younger generations in policymaking, that is what LEF stands for. A children's mayor is certainly part of that.

261834.explanation: Or even more often. The many committee meetings can also be held more frequently at various locations. This creates more connection between local government and residents.

261836.explanation: It is very important for us that starting entrepreneurs are supported, and this can be done in various ways. Of course, also through such discount schemes.

261837.explanation: If this is possible within European procurement rules. A local company is always our preference.

261838.explanation: Permits should generally be processed quickly and one should not have priority over another. However, with parties and festivals, there is a fixed deadline, so prompt action is necessary.

261839.explanation: Reducing residual waste should be rewarded. Treating it equally works counterproductively in this regard. We support the introduction of a weight-based fee.

261840.explanation: The terrace culture in Zutphen is thriving and meets a clear need. However, noise nuisance must be limited, for example by regulating terrace size and encouraging soundproof glass in homes through municipal subsidies.

261841.explanation: A fixed number of asylum seekers cannot be a rigid rule.

261842.explanation: This must be considered on a regional level, so that it can be determined where this can best take place.

261843.explanation: Everyone with urgency has the right to priority, including refugees. There are also too few shelter places for them. Only 8% of available social rental homes go to status holders. Refugees are not the problem; stagnating housing construction is.

261844.explanation: A fireworks show in a Natura 2000 area is incomprehensible. At the same time, we do support fun events. Therefore, we will continue to look for and discuss better, responsible alternatives before discarding the old ways.

261846.explanation: Investing in various forms of energy storage, such as batteries, is essential for an effective energy transition. This way everyone can participate, not just the affluent residents of our municipality.

261847.explanation: We support a better day and nightlife. Evening and night shelters are greatly lacking among young people.

261848.explanation: Simplicity in the collection system is what contributes most to good waste separation.

261849.explanation: Leasing municipal land is already subject to stricter rules, but implementation is difficult because some farmers have been there for a long time. That is why support for transition and phasing out the use of chemical agents is a priority for us.

261850.explanation: This is of course very desirable. If there are funds for an expensive underwater parking garage, we prefer to first invest in a full-fledged swimming pool that counts regionally.

261851.explanation: If the intention is not to create the largest Easter fire, we are positive about activities that connect people. An Easter fire can also be part of that.

261852.explanation: There are already rules for more nature. We support the 3-30-300 rule, also for older neighborhoods: a view of 3 trees, 30% tree canopy coverage, and within 300 meters a shaded spot, and in the long term we would like to see this supplemented with a drinking water point.

261853.explanation: The Hanzehuis can make a significant contribution to culture in Zutphen, with the third, smaller hall also being of great value. We would gladly discuss how the Hanzehuis can become a stronger part of the entire municipality, while maintaining support for other cultural initiatives in the municipality of Zutphen.

261854.explanation: We are advocates for more space for young people. A youth center is sorely needed in Zutphen, with a strong cultural day and night program. Additionally, more recreational spaces distributed throughout the municipality are desirable, not only for young people.

261855.explanation: This seems unnecessarily complicated. When the municipality lowers rents, it is up to the association members themselves to decide what membership fees they charge.

261856.explanation: This is very important for LEF. Although, of course, variation in every form of education improves livability for young people. It is important that we counteract outflow and aging; a university of applied sciences is part of the solution for this.

261857.explanation: Preschool language lessons for toddlers can be very important in some situations. Investment is definitely needed here, so that more children have a more equal chance.

261858.explanation: Enforcement is justified when this happens while the container is not full and functions properly. However, this should not apply when a container is full, jammed, or defective. You cannot expect residents with a small home without a garden to store their waste for a long time.

261859.explanation: Bronsbergen is a beautiful recreational area for Zutphen with lovely nature and vegetable gardens. That should also be cherished.

261860.explanation: Having a municipal housing company in Zutphen makes affordable housing possible, keeps rents public, and breaks the power of investors and slumlords. This promotes social justice, sustainability, and genuine public housing.

261861.explanation: For LEF this is an important issue. We want a vacancy fine based on property value. The more expensive/larger the property, the higher the fine. It should never be profitable to leave a property empty during a housing crisis.

261863.explanation: When there are enough affordable smaller homes, the municipality does not need to contribute. Facilitating flow is certainly necessary, but simply giving money will not work if there are no suitable homes.

261864.explanation: By building more cooperative housing forms, we encourage mixed living between elderly and young people, such as grandparents and grandchildren in the same neighborhood. Homes should also flexibly adapt to different life stages.

261865.explanation: Cooperative housing forms are the future. In our vision, only pleasant, collaborative neighborhoods will remain.

261866.explanation: Research shows that people living above the fifth floor feel significantly less connection with what happens on the street. LEF supports strengthening community formation. Such high-rise buildings do not fit well with this.

261867.explanation: We strongly support good protection for tenants, especially when landlords violate laws regarding proper landlord behavior. Whether a municipal rental team is sufficient remains to be seen.

261868.explanation: Low incomes often lead to chronic problematic debts, causing social exclusion and high societal costs. LEF Zutphen therefore advocates for debt forgiveness whenever possible so people can fully participate again.

261869.explanation: This should be a standard part of social participation and emancipation and should be just as self-evident in secondary education.

261871.explanation: Healthy nutrition for young people is truly essential. Of course, these should be organic vegetables and fruits.

261872.explanation: This is far too costly, while the parking lot behind the station still has sufficient capacity. An underground or underwater parking garage is therefore unnecessarily ambitious.

261873.explanation: It is incomprehensible that a city proud of its history and heritage has turned one of the oldest existing squares in the Netherlands into a parking lot. Therefore, we support the search for parking spots elsewhere and a dignified use of this beautiful square.

261874.explanation: This accessible dental care reaches children who would otherwise be excluded from care. Prevention and early detection strengthen equal opportunities and reduce later care costs.

261876.explanation: Prevention and detection by genuinely engaged youth workers is effective, inexpensive, and a great investment in the community. We want to deploy both experts by experience and youth workers on the streets and within educational institutions to support young people in daily life.

statements.title: The city centre must remain easily accessible by car.
The municipality should ask the police to more frequently check people riding fatbikes for antisocial behaviour.
At least 35% of newly built homes in the municipality of Zutphen should be social housing. In the municipality of Zutphen that figure is currently 30%.
statements.moreInfo: At the moment, for every new housing project in Zutphen at least 30% of the homes must be social rental housing.

statements.title: The municipality must hold a referendum before merging with another municipality.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet voor een fusie met een andere gemeente eerst een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.moreInfo: In a referendum a government puts a single-issue question to voters, who can vote for or against. In a national referendum all Dutch citizens aged 18 or older may vote. In a municipal referendum all residents of that municipality aged 18 or older may vote. Both the municipal council and ordinary citizens can request a referendum. If citizens want a referendum, they must collect signatures from others. The municipal rules state how many signatures are required. In the Netherlands the result of a referendum is not binding, which means a government does not have to follow the result and can make a different decision.

statements.title: A new residential neighborhood must be built in Bronsbergen.
statements.moreInfo: Bronsbergen is a rural area and a hamlet. It belongs to the South Districts.

statements.title: The speed limit should be reduced to 30 km/h on more streets.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality of Zutphen must set up its own housing company to build and rent homes.
statements.moreInfo: In Zutphen housing corporations such as Ieder-1 and Sint Elisabeth rent out homes. They are independent but do make agreements with the municipality about goals they want to achieve. The municipality of Zutphen can also set up its own housing corporation (or housing company). Although allowed, creating a new municipal housing company is legally and financially complex and is often not the first choice compared with cooperating with existing housing corporations. There are municipalities in the Netherlands that have their own housing company.

statements.title: There should be a children's mayor in the municipality of Zutphen.
statements.moreInfo: A children's mayor stands up for the interests of children. The children's mayor tells the municipal council what children find important: what are their ideas to make the municipality safe and healthy? A children's mayor is usually appointed for one year and is in group 7 or 8.

statements.title: An application for a permit for a party or festival should be given priority over other permit applications.
The municipality should give money to elderly people who move out of a large family home.
For residents receiving debt assistance, the municipality should take over all debts as soon as possible; residents would then only have to repay one debt to the municipality.
The grey bin for residual waste should remain in Zutphen.
The municipality should invest in a campaign to attract a university of applied sciences (hbo) to Zutphen.
statements.moreInfo: At the moment there is no hbo (university of applied sciences) program in Zutphen. The nearest hbo institutions are in Deventer, Apeldoorn and Arnhem.

statements.title: Camera surveillance should be installed in the Schupstoel.
statements.moreInfo: On the Schupstoel, a street in the center of Zutphen, several streets with cafés converge, such as the Vaaltstraat and the Beukerstraat. Some people feel unsafe there in the evenings. Camera surveillance is one option to increase the feeling of safety.

statements.title: The municipality should have a rental team that helps tenants with problems with their landlord, such as excessive rent.
Large new-build projects must always include homes suitable for live-in grandparents.
The municipality should allow cafés and restaurants to keep their terraces open for longer.
statements.moreInfo: In the municipality of Zutphen terraces may be open daily from 08:00 to 24:00. A terrace in the city center may be open until 01:00 from April to October.

statements.title: The municipality should employ more youth workers who help young people with mental health problems.
The municipality should invest in lessons on managing money wisely for refugees who are allowed to stay in the Netherlands.
The municipal enforcement officers (boa's) who monitor safety in Zutphen should be given a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in de gemeente Zutphen controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should provide a dental bus that visits schools.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social rental housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: The municipality should abandon the construction of the cultural center Het Hanzehuis.
statements.moreInfo: The Hanzehuis is the new name for the Hanzehof. It is currently a theatre. The municipality wants to renovate and expand the Hanzehof. The Hanzehuis will also house the library. There will also be more space for music performances, lectures and exhibitions.

statements.title: 's Gravenhof should be made car-free.
statements.moreInfo: The 's Gravenhof is a square by the town hall where people can park their cars.

statements.title: The municipality should not charge property tax (OZB) for the commercial buildings of start-ups for the first three years.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de eerste drie jaar geen

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

titleParts.text: (OZB) vragen voor het bedrijfsgebouw van startende ondernemingen.

statements.title: The municipality should ban the use of wood-burning stoves and open fireplaces when weather conditions make it unsuitable.
No more asylum seekers may be accommodated in the municipality of Zutphen than the 740 who are there now.

titleParts.text: Er mogen in de gemeente Zutphen niet meer

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

statements.title: The municipal council should meet once a year in a neighborhood or community center.
The municipality of Zutphen should ban Easter fires.

The municipality of Zutphen needs more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who deal with public safety.

The municipality must provide an extra location for temporary shelter for asylum seekers.
The municipality must ensure that community centers can also be open in the evenings.
The municipality must ask the police to hold a weekly consultation hour with the neighborhood officer in every district.
The municipality must stop the fireworks show during the fair.
The maximum height for apartment blocks must remain eight storeys (25 meters).
Organic farmers should be given priority when leasing municipal agricultural land.
The municipality must expand the De IJselslag swimming pool with a play area and an outdoor pool.
There must be a youth center in the municipality of Zutphen.
No underground parking garage may be built in Zutphen.
The municipality must invest in extra language lessons for toddlers with a language delay.
Owners who leave their home vacant for more than a year should be fined.
Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) should issue fines more often for placing waste next to underground waste containers.
The municipality of Zutphen should designate locations where residents can build homes together.
The municipality should give households with infants aged 6–18 months a free bag of vegetables for the baby for eight weeks.
